```ts
    await $`gh pr create --title ${"Add login"} --head spry/dondenton/aaa11111 --base main --body ${"Login"}`
      .cwd(repo.path)
      .quiet();
    await $`gh pr create --title ${"Add logout"} --head spry/dondenton/bbb22222 --base spry/dondenton/aaa11111 --body ${"Logout"}`
      .cwd(repo.path)

```


Run the doc-generation step the repo uses (doc tests are the source of truth). Confirm which script generates `docs/generated/`:

```bash
grep -n "generate\|docs:generate\|doc" package.json | head
```

Then run that script (e.g. `bun run docs:generate` if present). Expected: `docs/generated/commands/land.md` (and siblings) regenerated, reflecting the no-retarget prose.

- [ ] **Step 4: Replay to confirm offline determinism**


title: EPIC: sync check read/write split (land verifies, never retargets)
description:
# Design: `checkSync` — separating read from write in sync/land

**Date:** 2026-07-12
**Status:** Approved (design)
**Related:** epic `spry-dzp` (land no-retarget), task `spry-dzp.5` (cassette re-record)

## Problem

`sp land` currently embeds the full mutating `syncCommand` as its first step:

```ts
// land.ts step 1
await syncCommand(ctx, { cwd });
```

`syncCommand` performs writes mid-flow — in particular `retargetMismatched`,
which issues `gh pr edit <n> --base <expected>` to stack PRs onto their proper
bases. When land runs this against a freshly-set-up stack (bottom PR on `main`,
upper PR also on `main`), the embedded sync retargets the upper PR to its stacked
base. **That base change triggers a fresh `pull_request` CI run on GitHub**, which
is still `pending` moments later when land's own readiness gate queries PR state —
so land aborts on "CI checks are still running." The old land cassette only passed
by timing luck (the re-triggered run happened to be green when queried).

### Root cause (confirmed)

The race is structural, not a flake: **land mutates PR bases (via embedded sync)
and then immediately gates on CI status of those same PRs.** A base change is a CI
trigger; gating on CI right after guarantees a pending window. It is not
`spry-xil` (stale refs) or `spry-bei` (main pollution) — those did not recur.

## Goal

`sp land` must **verify** readiness — including proper PR targeting — and **fail**
(directing the user to `sp sync`) when anything is not ready. It must not perform
the retarget itself, so it can never trigger the CI run it then waits on.

This requires separating sync's **read** from its **write**.

## Design

The split has two internal functions (no user-facing `sp check` command):
**`checkSync`** acquires remote state and persists it to refs; **`analyzeStack`**
is a pure comparison over those refs. Both `land` and `sync` run `checkSync` then
`analyzeStack`, and differ only in how they respond to the analysis.

### `checkSync` — acquire remote state into refs

`checkSync` makes the remote state locally readable via refs, and does **nothing
else** — no commit mutation, no branch push, no PR create, no PR retarget:

```
checkSync(ctx, opts) →
  1. git fetch      // updates refs/remotes/origin/* (branch tips) — git persists these
  2. fetch group records + PR cache from remote
  3. parse the stack (group-aware) into units   // does NOT inject IDs
  4. findPRsForBranches(scopeBranches)           // live PR state from GitHub
  5. writePRCache(...)                           // persist PR state to refs/spry/prs
```

The two halves of "remote truth" are both **ref-backed** after `checkSync`:
branch tips live in `refs/remotes/origin/*` (git owns this persistence via the
fetch — a real `git fetch`, not `ls-remote`, which prints without persisting), and
PR state lives in `refs/spry/prs` (spry owns this). This is the shape-A decision:
the PR cache always advances to what GitHub reports, even when a caller
subsequently decides the state is not landable.

### `analyzeStack` — pure comparison over refs

`analyzeStack` reads only local git refs — no network, no I/O — and returns a
per-unit verdict. It **assumes a fetch has happened** (i.e. `checkSync` ran, or is
otherwise as fresh as the last fetch) and compares SHAs directly:

```
analyzeStack(localStack, config) →   // reads local commits, origin/*, refs/spry/prs
  per unit: {
    missingId?    // any commit in the unit lacks a Spry-Commit-Id (local commits only)
    unpushed?     // unit.localTip !== rev-parse(origin/<branch>); unresolved ref ⇒ unpushed
    misTargeted?  // cachedPR.baseRefName !== expectedBaseFor(unit)
    readiness     // evaluateReadiness over the cached PR (missing / failing / pending / review)
  }
```

It runs **anytime**: on a brand-new branch, `origin/<branch>` does not resolve and
`refs/spry/prs` has no entry, so every unit is flagged (unpushed, no PR) — the
correct degradation, falling out of the ref reads with no special-casing. Its
freshness is exactly "as of the last fetch"; callers that need current truth run
`checkSync` first. The **missing-ID scan** is a small pure helper over local
commits, shared with the inject function(s) (which answer the same
"which commits need an ID?" question before any remote state exists).

### `sp sync` = inject → check → analyze → apply writes

Sync keeps ownership of every mutation, expressed as **responses to
`analyzeStack`'s verdict**:

```
syncCommand:
  1. injectMissingIds                 // sync FIXES missing IDs (write; before check)
  2. checkSync(...)                   // acquire remote state into refs
  3. analysis = analyzeStack(...)     // pure comparison
  4. apply writes per flag:
       - unpushed    → pushBranch
       - (--open) unit with no PR + selected → createPR
       - misTargeted → retargetPR
  5. re-run checkSync's PR-cache write   // reflect the writes just made
```

Injection stays in sync, **before** `checkSync`, because neither `checkSync` nor
`analyzeStack` may mutate commits.

### `sp land` = check → analyze → assert ready → ff-push only

Land injects nothing and writes nothing until the final trunk fast-forward:

```
landCommand:
  1. checkSync(...)                   // acquire remote state into refs
  2. resolve --through scope (or TUI pick)
  3. analysis = analyzeStack(...)     // same pure comparison sync uses
  4. FAIL → "run `sp sync`" if any unit WITHIN THE LANDING SCOPE has a flag:
       missingId | unpushed | misTargeted | readiness-not-ok
  5. ff-push trunk to the target tip        // the ONLY land write
  6. cleanup tail (unchanged: scrub PR cache, group records, opt-in branch delete)
```

Land only gates on the units it is actually landing — those in the `--through`
scope (bottom → the target unit). Flags on units **above** the land point are
irrelevant: land isn't touching those PRs, so an unpushed or mis-targeted unit
higher in the stack must not block a landable bottom slice. `analyzeStack` reports
the whole stack; land filters the verdict to its scope before deciding.

The asymmetry, stated plainly: **sync fixes; land demands.** Both read the same
`analyzeStack` verdict. Sync responds to each flag with the matching write across
the whole stack; land treats a flag **on an in-scope unit** as a hard failure the
user must resolve with `sp sync` first. Because land no longer retargets, it never
triggers the CI run it gates on — the race is gone by construction.

## Components

### New / changed

- **`checkSync` (in `src/commands/sync.ts`, exported)** — acquires remote state
  into refs: `git fetch` (branch tips → `refs/remotes/origin/*`), fetch group
  records + PR cache, `findPRsForBranches`, `writePRCache`. Its only mutation is
  the PR cache. Replaces sync's current `listRemoteBranches` (`ls-remote`) usage —
  a real fetch is required so `analyzeStack` can read tips from `origin/*`.
- **`analyzeStack` (new module, e.g. `src/commands/stack-analysis.ts`, pure)** —
  reads local commits, `refs/remotes/origin/<branch>`, and `refs/spry/prs`;
  returns the per-unit verdict `{ missingId?, unpushed?, misTargeted?, readiness }`.
  No network, no I/O, runnable anytime. Consumed by both `sync` and `land`.
  Absorbs the comparison logic that was inline in `pushExistingBranches` /
  `retargetMismatched`, plus land's readiness gate — one definition of
  "local vs remote diff." `expectedBaseFor` moves here (or a shared helper) since
  both the `misTargeted` flag and sync's retarget response use it.
- **missing-ID scan** — a small pure helper over local commits, shared by
  `analyzeStack` (the `missingId` flag) and the inject function(s) (which decide
  which commits to stamp). Single source of "which commits lack a Spry-Commit-Id."
- **`syncCommand`** — refactored to `inject → checkSync → analyzeStack → apply
writes → re-cache`. The write helpers (`pushExistingBranches`, `openPRs`,
  `retargetMismatched`, `writePRCache`) are retained but now _respond to_ the
  analysis rather than re-deriving it inline.
- **`landCommand`** — drops `await syncCommand(...)`; runs `checkSync` then
  `analyzeStack` over the scope; **fails** (→ `sp sync`) on any flag; keeps the
  ff-push and cleanup tail as-is.
- **`evaluateReadiness` (`src/commands/land-readiness.ts`)** — unchanged; called by
  `analyzeStack` to produce the `readiness` portion of each unit's verdict.

### `--all` path

`syncAllCommand` / `finishSyncAll` _should_ eventually follow the same shape
(`checkSync` acquires, `analyzeStack` compares, writes respond), but this is
**deferred**: the first implementation keeps `--all` functionally unchanged and
correct, and converges it in a follow-up. The land race fix does not depend on
`--all` being refactored. No behavior change for `--all` users in either case.

## Error handling

- **`checkSync`** surfaces GitHub-unreachable via the existing
  `retargetingFallbackHint` / `classifyGhInfraError` machinery. If PR lookup fails,
  the PR cache isn't refreshed and `analyzeStack` reads stale/absent PR state:
  sync warns and skips retarget (as today); land **fails** (it cannot assert
  readiness without trustworthy PR state).
- **Land failure messages** are explicit and name the offending commit / branch /
  PR per flag, each ending in "Run `sp sync` and try again." The `missingId` flag
  is a friendly pre-check, never left to surface as a raw parse/resolution error.
- Land's cleanup tail is unchanged: still best-effort, still warns-and-continues,
  still never aborts a completed land.

## Testing

- **Unit** — `analyzeStack` is the primary unit-test target: drive it with
  synthetic local commits + `origin/*` refs + PR cache and assert each flag
  (`missingId`, `unpushed`, `misTargeted`, `readiness`) fires and clears correctly,
  including the brand-new-branch all-flags case. `checkSync` tested for "no
  mutation beyond PR cache" (asserts no push / create / retarget on the gh seam).
- **Doc tests** — `land.doc.test.ts` fragments 010/020 unchanged in intent. The
  setup currently opens the upper PR mis-based on `main`; under the new design land
  will now **fail** that with a mis-targeted assertion, since land no longer fixes
  targeting. Setup is therefore changed to open each PR **already stacked on its
  correct base** (bottom → `main`, upper → the bottom unit's branch) via
  `gh pr create --base <stacked-base>`, so the stack is landable without any
  in-land write. This matches the real workflow (a user runs `sp sync` to stack
  the PRs, then `sp land`) and — critically — means **setup never changes a PR
  base after the CI run starts**, so the pending-CI race cannot recur even in the
  test fixture.
- **Cassette re-record (`spry-dzp.5`)** — with land no longer retargeting, its gh
  traffic is pure reads (PR lookups) + no `pr edit`. Re-record both land cassettes
  (`commands__land--010.json`, `--020.json`) in `SPRY_RECORD=1`; the pending-CI
  race that broke the earlier record run cannot occur because land triggers no CI.
  Regenerate `docs/generated/commands/land.*` and commit cassettes + docs.

## Out of scope

- No user-facing `sp check` command ("check" is internal only).
- No change to PR body management, `--open` selection UX, or cleanup semantics.
- No change to `sp rebase` (injection-for-fix stays a sync concern; rebase is
  unrelated).
close_reason: Epic complete: sync read/write split shipped. checkSync+analyzeStack+landBlockers; land verifies & fails to sp sync, no embedded sync/retarget; pinned push lease; cassettes re-recorded (zero gh pr edit). All 10 tasks + spry-s2tx done. spry-dzp.5 validated.

title: Brainstorm+implement: converge sp sync --all onto checkSync/analyzeStack (+ pinned lease)
description:
Brainstorm + implement: converge the `sp sync --all` path onto the checkSync/analyzeStack read/write split.

BACKGROUND. The sync read/write split (merged in PR #24, epic spry-sync-check-split-mfw, now closed) refactored the SINGLE-branch `syncCommand` to flow through two new internal functions in src/commands/:
- `checkSync(ctx, {cwd})` in sync.ts — acquires remote state into refs (real `git fetch` so refs/remotes/origin/* is fresh, group-record + PR-cache fetch, stack parse, findPRsForBranches, writePRCache). Mutates only the PR cache. Also snapshots pre-fetch remote-tracking tips into `preFetchRemoteTips` (for the pinned push lease).
- `analyzeStack(ctx, {units, commits, prCache, config}, {cwd})` in stack-analysis.ts — pure per-unit verdict {missingId, unpushed, misTargeted, currentBase, expectedBase}. Plus `landBlockers`, `expectedBaseFor`, `missingIdHashes`.
The single-branch flow is now: inject IDs -> checkSync -> (derive writes: push/open/retarget) -> re-cache. Pushes pin `--force-with-lease` to the pre-fetch tracking sha (see src/gh/push.ts `leaseExpectedSha`).

WHAT WAS DEFERRED. `syncAllCommand` / `finishSyncAll` (also in src/commands/sync.ts) were intentionally NOT converted in that epic, to keep the PR focused on the land race fix. They still use the OLDER shape: per-branch inject + `pushExistingBranches` against a `listRemoteBranches` (ls-remote) map + `retargetMismatched`, and they pass an EMPTY Map for `preFetchRemoteTips` (so `--all` pushes fall back to BARE `--force-with-lease`, not the pinned lease). `--all` is documented as push-only (never opens PRs, never rebases, cannot combine with --open).

THE WORK (this bead). First BRAINSTORM the design (use the brainstorming skill), because there are real choices:
1. Should `--all` do a real `git fetch` per stack (or one fetch up front) so it can use analyzeStack + the pinned lease like the single-branch path? Today `--all` deliberately avoids the fetch (hence bare lease). Weigh the multi-stack cost of fetching vs. the clobber-safety gain. Note the pinned-lease safety fix (spry-s2tx) and the ramifications-review bead spry-lease-ramifications-review-d2yl — coordinate: converging --all likely means --all pushes should ALSO pin the lease, which is exactly one of the push paths that review must cover.
2. Should each stack run checkSync (which is single-stack-shaped: it loads config, parses the CURRENT stack) or does --all need a multi-stack variant of the acquire step? finishSyncAll currently does ONE batched findPRsForBranches across all stacks and ONE writePRCache — preserve that batching (don't regress to N fetches/N cache writes) while still feeding analyzeStack per stack.
3. Keep `--all` push-only semantics (no analyzeStack-driven "open" or "retarget-only-when-flagged" behavior change that would surprise users). The goal is internal convergence + lease pinning, not new --all behavior.

ACCEPTANCE:
- `sp sync --all` uses the same analyzeStack verdict + pinned-lease push path as single-branch sync (no bare-lease fallback except genuine first-publish / no-tracking-ref cases).
- Batched PR lookup + single combined writePRCache across all stacks preserved.
- No behavior change visible to `--all` users beyond safer pushes.
- Existing `--all` tests still pass; add a test that an `--all` push pins the lease (mirrors the single-branch stale-ref guard test in tests/commands/sync.test.ts).
- tsc clean, oxlint/oxfmt clean, full `bun test` green.

FILES: src/commands/sync.ts (syncAllCommand, finishSyncAll, pushExistingBranches signature already threads preFetchTips), src/commands/stack-analysis.ts (reuse analyzeStack; no change expected), src/gh/push.ts (leaseExpectedSha already exists). Tests in tests/commands/sync.test.ts.

Design context lives in the (closed) epic spry-sync-check-split-mfw and PR #24; the roadmap note about deferral is in docs/rebase-roadmap? no — it was only in the epic, which is why this bead exists.

title: sp sync: reorder retarget marks PRs merged (two-phase park-on-trunk fix)
description:
PROBLEM: When commits in a stack are REORDERED and then `sp sync`ed, the stacked-PR bases are re-adjusted in a single in-order pass (retargetMismatched in src/commands/sync.ts ~lines 365-393, driven by expectedBaseFor at ~line 355). Because the target/base branches are not readjusted in the right order relative to the push, PRs can transiently end up with their head reachable from the default (trunk) branch, and GitHub then marks them MERGED (GitHub marks a PR MERGED on reachability from the default branch, not on its base). Net effect after a reorder-sync: all the PRs in the stack show as merged.

WHY IT HAPPENS: retargetMismatched walks `units` in order and, for each PR whose baseRefName != expectedBaseFor(unit), calls retargetPR to the new expected base. After a reorder, the expected bases form a different chain than what is currently on GitHub. Retargeting one PR at a time, interleaved with (or after) a push that has already moved branch tips, means intermediate base/head combinations can make a PR's commits look reachable from trunk. There is no ordering discipline that guarantees no PR is ever transiently based such that it appears merged.

PROPOSED FIX (escapable via a two-phase approach): 
1. DETECT reorder: before retargeting, recognize whether the PR bases have been reordered (current on-GitHub base chain differs from the newly-expected base chain from expectedBaseFor over the stack order).
2. PARK: if reordered, first retarget ALL affected PRs' bases to the trunk/main branch.
3. PUSH: push the reordered branch tips.
4. FINAL RETARGET: then retarget each PR to its correct new stacked base (expectedBaseFor), in order.

Parking everyone on trunk first breaks the transient reachability chains so no PR is momentarily reachable-from-trunk-via-its-base during the shuffle, then the final retarget rebuilds the correct stacked chain.

RELATED: this is adjacent to spry-4k6 / spry-8lz (sp LAND no-retarget fix), but distinct — that work removes retargeting from `sp land`; this issue is about `sp sync`'s retarget ORDER when commits are reordered. The land fix leaves retargetPR in place precisely because sync still needs it (src/commands/sync.ts:34, :384).

FILES: src/commands/sync.ts — retargetMismatched (~365-393), expectedBaseFor (~355-363), the push+retarget sequencing around lines 144-167 and 515-555. Tests: tests/commands/sync.test.ts (+ sync.doc.test.ts / cassette re-record if gh traffic changes).

NEEDS: confirm the exact reachability/merge-marking mechanism with a reproduction (reorder a 2-3 PR stack, sync, observe all PRs flip MERGED) before implementing; design the reorder-detection predicate; decide whether park-on-trunk is unconditional on any reorder or only when a specific unsafe transition is detected.
close_reason: Fix merged to main via PR #25 (merge commit f370db3). sp sync parks mismatched PRs to trunk before push, then re-stacks (single + --all). Root cause = the push (proven by live repro), not the retarget. Validated on real GitHub. Follow-ups: spry-az6j, spry-0xrq, spry-8vz2.

title: Design: sp sync reorder-merge fix (full spec)
description:
# Fix `sp sync`: reorder marks PRs MERGED (spry-206)

**Date:** 2026-07-12
**Bead:** spry-206
**Status:** Design approved, ready for implementation plan

## Problem

When commits in a stack are **reordered** and then `sp sync`ed, PRs in the stack
can be marked **MERGED** by GitHub — corrupting the record irreversibly. A
reorder-then-sync can flip one or more PRs to MERGED depending on the reorder
shape.

## Root cause (proven by live reproduction against `spry-check`)

Two candidate triggers were tested in isolation:

| Operation                                                                                                                      | Behavior when a PR head becomes reachable from the base                                                                               | Verdict                            |
| ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------- |
| **PUSH** — force-push a base branch to a tip that now contains a child PR's head, while the child PR still points at that base | GitHub's push webhook marks the child PR **MERGED**                                                                                   | **This is the bug.** Irreversible. |
| **RETARGET** — `gh pr edit --base X` where X already contains the head                                                         | GitHub **rejects** the edit: `"There are no new commits between base branch 'X' and head branch 'Y'"`. PR stays OPEN, base unchanged. | Harmless. Never the trigger.       |

Reproduction detail (stack A→B→C, reordered to C,A,B so C becomes the bottom):
after the reorder, branch `sp/B`'s new tip is `main→C→A→B`, which **contains
commit C**. Force-pushing `sp/B` while PR-C still has base `sp/B` made PR-C's
head reachable from `sp/B` → GitHub marked **PR-C MERGED**. The subsequent
retarget could not even run: `"Cannot change the base branch of a closed pull
request."`

**Conclusion:** the push is the sole trigger; retarget can only ever fail
loudly. The current `sp sync` order is **push → retarget**, so the push happens
while PR bases are still at their pre-reorder positions — exactly the dangerous
window.

## Current flow (the bug)

`syncCommand` (`src/commands/sync.ts`):

1. inject commit IDs
2. `checkSync` — fetch + parse + PR lookup (`prMap`) + PR-cache refresh
3. list remote branches
4. **push** existing branches to new tips ← **danger: bases still stale here**
5. `--open` — open new PRs (own pushes)
6. **retarget** mismatched PRs (`retargetMismatched`)

`syncAllCommand` → `finishSyncAll` has the **same** bug: it pushes every stack's
branches (step 3 of its loop) and only fetches `prMap` + retargets _after_ the
push (`finishSyncAll`).

## The fix: three-phase sync, gated on a reorder predicate

Insert a **pre-push park** phase before the push. The corrected order for the
single-stack path:

1. inject commit IDs
2. `checkSync` (unchanged — provides `prMap` with each PR's current
   `baseRefName`)
3. list remote branches
4. **Phase 1 — park (NEW):** _if a reorder is detected_, retarget every open PR
   whose base is changing to **`config.trunk`**. Trunk never contains a stack
   head, so these edits always succeed.
5. **Phase 2 — push:** existing push phase, unchanged. With every affected base
   now on trunk, no push can make a head reachable from its base → zero merges.
6. `--open` — unchanged (see interactions).
7. **Phase 3 — re-stack:** existing `retargetMismatched`, unchanged. After the
   park, mismatched PRs sit on trunk, so this re-stacks them to their final
   stacked bases.

   Note on `retargetMismatched`'s stale-`prMap` read: it compares each PR's
   `baseRefName` **as of `checkSync`** (i.e. the _pre-park_ base) against the
   final `expected` base, and retargets when they differ. This is correct for
   the reorder case: for a moved unit, pre-park-base ≠ final-base, so the
   skip does not fire and the PR is retargeted to its final base (the park's
   intermediate trunk base is invisible to this comparison but irrelevant — the
   call sets the final base directly). Implementation note: a unit whose
   _final_ expected base is trunk and whose _pre-park_ base was also trunk is
   correctly skipped; a unit whose final base is trunk but pre-park base was
   not will issue one retarget-to-trunk that duplicates the park (harmless).
   The validated repro exercises this path and ends correctly stacked.

### Reorder-detection predicate (gates Phase 1)

For each unit that has an **open** PR in `prMap`:

- `currentBase = prMap.get(branch).baseRefName`
- `expectedBase = expectedBaseFor(unit, units, config)`

If **every** open PR has `currentBase === expectedBase`, nothing is moving —
this is a normal amend/top-push sync. **Phase 1 is skipped** (today's cheap
path, no extra `gh` calls). Phase 1 activates only when at least one open PR's
base is changing.

### Parking scope: park-all-mismatched

Phase 1 parks **every** open PR whose `currentBase !== expectedBase` to trunk
(not a narrower "only endangered" subset).

Rationale: a base-branch change does **not** trigger CI (GitHub Actions
`pull_request` events fire on `opened`/`reopened`/`synchronize` — i.e. head
changes — not on base edits). The only CI-triggering event is the force-push,
which happens exactly once regardless. So park-all-mismatched costs only a few
extra `gh pr edit` calls (fast, no CI) and avoids the subtle reachability
computation a narrower scope would require. This scope was validated
end-to-end.

### Validation (end-to-end repro, two shapes)

The full three-phase flow (park-all→trunk, push new order, re-stack) was run
against `spry-check` for two reorder shapes:

- **Shape 1 — top→bottom** (A,B,C → C,A,B)
- **Shape 2 — full reversal** (A,B,C → C,B,A)

Both ended with **all PRs OPEN** and **correct final stacked bases**. No PR was
marked MERGED at any phase.

## Interactions

### `--open`

Newly-opened PRs (step 6) are created fresh with their correct stacked base,
and their branches are first-published to brand-new refs. A brand-new branch
push cannot retroactively merge a pre-existing PR, and a just-created PR's base
is already correct — so **newly-opened PRs need no parking**. Phase 1 reads
`prMap` (pre-sync GitHub state), which only contains PRs that already exist, so
this is handled naturally.

### `--all`

`syncAllCommand` needs the same pre-push park applied **per stack** before its
push loop. Today `finishSyncAll` fetches `prMap` only _after_ pushing; the fix
requires PR bases _before_ the push, so `--all` must move (or add) its PR
lookup earlier — before the per-stack push loop. Then each stack parks its
mismatched open PRs to trunk before that stack is pushed. The existing
post-loop retarget in `finishSyncAll` becomes Phase 3.

### Failure handling (fail-safe)

If a Phase-1 park retarget fails (network/gh error), the affected branches must
**not** be pushed — pushing with an unparked stale base risks the merge. A park
failure therefore aborts the push for the affected branch(es), surfaces a
warning, and flips `hadFailure` so sync exits non-zero, consistent with existing
warning handling.

## Files touched

- `src/commands/sync.ts`
  - `syncCommand`: insert Phase-1 park before `pushExistingBranches`; gate on
    the reorder predicate; wire park failures into the push gating +
    `hadFailure`.
  - `syncAllCommand` / `finishSyncAll`: move/add the PR lookup before the
    per-stack push loop; park each stack's mismatched open PRs to trunk before
    pushing it.
  - New helper (e.g. `parkMismatchedToTrunk`) parallel to `retargetMismatched`,
    plus a `detectReorder` / `hasBaseChange` predicate. `expectedBaseFor` is
    reused from `./stack-analysis.ts`.
- Tests: `tests/commands/sync.test.ts` — unit tests for the predicate + park
  helper + push-gating-on-park-failure. Cassette/doc coverage
  (`tests/commands/sync.doc.test.ts`) for a reorder sync if `gh` traffic
  changes (re-record with `SPRY_RECORD=1`).
- Docs: `CHANGELOG.md` `[Unreleased]`; bead spry-206 updated with corrected
  root cause; `docs/rebuild-roadmap.md` if a sync decision note is warranted.

## Non-goals

- No change to `sp land` (its no-retarget fix is spry-4k6/spry-8lz, separate).
- No behind-trunk guard (deferred, per roadmap).
- No PR body/title management changes.
- No new config knobs — the three-phase flow is automatic and self-gating.
close_reason: Design reference; fix implemented and merged via PR #25.

title: Plan: sp sync reorder-merge fix (context + self-review)
description:
# `sp sync` reorder-merge fix — Implementation Plan

> **For agentic workers:** REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers:subagent-driven-development (recommended) or superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan task-by-task. Steps use checkbox (`- [ ]`) syntax for tracking.

**Goal:** Stop `sp sync` from marking stacked PRs MERGED when commits are reordered, by parking every mismatched open PR onto trunk before the force-push, then re-stacking.

**Architecture:** Insert a pre-push "park" phase into `syncCommand` (and the per-stack loop in `syncAllCommand`), gated on a reorder predicate that compares each open PR's current GitHub base against its expected stacked base. When any base is changing, all mismatched open PRs are retargeted to `config.trunk` first; only then does the push run, followed by the existing re-stack retarget. A park failure blocks the push for the affected branch (fail-safe) and makes sync exit non-zero.

**Tech Stack:** TypeScript on Bun. Tests via `bun test`. Real-git test repos via `createRepo`, `gh` stubbed via `stubGh`; doc/cassette tests replay recorded `gh` traffic offline.

**Design doc:** `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-12-sync-reorder-merge-fix-design.md`

---

## File structure

- **`src/commands/sync.ts`** — the only source file changed.
  - New pure helper `stackHasReorder(units, prMap, config)` — the reorder predicate.
  - New async helper `parkMismatchedToTrunk(ctx, config, units, branches, prMap, cwd)` — retargets mismatched open PRs to trunk; returns the set of branches that failed to park.
  - `syncCommand` — call the predicate; if a reorder is detected, park before push; exclude park-failed branches from the push; fold park failures into `hadFailure`.
  - `syncAllCommand` / `finishSyncAll` — move the batched PR lookup to _before_ the per-stack push loop so each stack can park before it is pushed.
- **`tests/commands/sync.test.ts`** — unit tests for the predicate, the park phase (park edits target trunk and precede the re-stack edits), park-failure-blocks-push, and the non-reorder no-park path. `syncAllCommand` park coverage.
- **`tests/commands/sync.doc.test.ts`** + **`tests/fixtures/cassettes/commands__sync--070.json`** — a doc/cassette test that reorders a real 2-PR stack against `spry-check` and asserts both PRs stay OPEN. Cassette recorded with `SPRY_RECORD=1` (agent-run).
- **`CHANGELOG.md`** — `[Unreleased]` entry.

Existing helpers reused (no signature changes):

- `expectedBaseFor(unit, units, config): string` from `./stack-analysis.ts`.
- `retargetPR(ctx, prNumber, newBase, { cwd }): Promise<void>` from `../gh/index.ts` (throws on failure).
- `branchForUnit(unit, config): string`, `PRInfo` (`{ number, state, baseRefName, ... }`).

---


---

## Self-review notes

- **Spec coverage:** predicate (Task 1) ✓, park-all-mismatched to trunk (Task 2) ✓, three-phase order in `syncCommand` (Task 3) ✓, fail-safe park-blocks-push (Tasks 3–4) ✓, `--all` early PR lookup + per-stack park (Task 5) ✓, `--open` needs no parking (handled implicitly — park reads pre-sync `prMap`; no task needed, noted here) ✓, cassette/doc real-gh validation (Task 7) ✓, changelog (Task 8) ✓.
- **Phase-3 stale-`prMap` note from the spec:** `retargetMismatched` is unchanged and reused; it compares pre-park base vs final expected, which re-stacks parked PRs correctly. The harmless redundant "re-stack to trunk" edge (final base = trunk, pre-park base ≠ trunk) is accepted, matching the spec.
- **Type consistency:** `parkMismatchedToTrunk` returns `Set<string>` in Task 2 and is consumed as `Set<string>` (`parkFailed`) in Tasks 3 & 5. `pushExistingBranches`' new `skip: ReadonlySet<string>` param accepts that `Set`. `finishSyncAll`'s new `prMap` param type `Map<string, PRInfo | null> | undefined` matches `findPRsForBranches`' return.
- **No placeholders:** all steps carry real code/commands.

```

```
close_reason: Plan reference; all 9 tasks executed and merged via PR #25.

title: Task 1: Reorder-detection predicate (stackHasReorder)
Task 2: parkMismatchedToTrunk helper
Task 3: Wire park phase into syncCommand
Task 4: Park-failure blocks push (fail-safe test)
Task 5: Apply park to sp sync --all

## Task 8: Changelog

**Files:**

- Modify: `CHANGELOG.md` (`[Unreleased]`)

- [ ] **Step 1: Add the entry**

Under `[Unreleased]` → `### Fixed` (create the heading if absent):

```markdown
### Fixed

- `sp sync` no longer marks stacked PRs as merged when commits are reordered.
  A reorder made a PR's head reachable from its stale base branch during the
  force-push, which GitHub treated as a merge. Sync now parks every affected
  open PR onto trunk before pushing, then re-stacks them.
```

```bash
git add CHANGELOG.md
git commit -m "docs(changelog): note sync reorder-merge fix (spry-206)"
```

```markdown
  A reorder made a PR's head reachable from its stale base branch during the
  force-push, which GitHub treated as a merge. Sync now parks every affected
  open PR onto trunk before pushing, then re-stacks them.
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Commit**

```bash
git add CHANGELOG.md
git commit -m "docs(changelog): note sync reorder-merge fix (spry-206)"
```

---
close_reason: Changelog Fixed entry (added with Task 3 per per-commit rule) verified accurate; extended to note --all park protection. Commit 629e0cb.

title: Task 9: Manual verification with verify skill
description:
# `sp sync` reorder-merge fix — Implementation Plan

> **For agentic workers:** REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers:subagent-driven-development (recommended) or superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan task-by-task. Steps use checkbox (`- [ ]`) syntax for tracking.

**Goal:** Stop `sp sync` from marking stacked PRs MERGED when commits are reordered, by parking every mismatched open PR onto trunk before the force-push, then re-stacking.

**Architecture:** Insert a pre-push "park" phase into `syncCommand` (and the per-stack loop in `syncAllCommand`), gated on a reorder predicate that compares each open PR's current GitHub base against its expected stacked base. When any base is changing, all mismatched open PRs are retargeted to `config.trunk` first; only then does the push run, followed by the existing re-stack retarget. A park failure blocks the push for the affected branch (fail-safe) and makes sync exit non-zero.

**Tech Stack:** TypeScript on Bun. Tests via `bun test`. Real-git test repos via `createRepo`, `gh` stubbed via `stubGh`; doc/cassette tests replay recorded `gh` traffic offline.

**Design doc:** `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-12-sync-reorder-merge-fix-design.md`

---

## File structure

- **`src/commands/sync.ts`** — the only source file changed.
  - New pure helper `stackHasReorder(units, prMap, config)` — the reorder predicate.
  - New async helper `parkMismatchedToTrunk(ctx, config, units, branches, prMap, cwd)` — retargets mismatched open PRs to trunk; returns the set of branches that failed to park.
  - `syncCommand` — call the predicate; if a reorder is detected, park before push; exclude park-failed branches from the push; fold park failures into `hadFailure`.
  - `syncAllCommand` / `finishSyncAll` — move the batched PR lookup to _before_ the per-stack push loop so each stack can park before it is pushed.
- **`tests/commands/sync.test.ts`** — unit tests for the predicate, the park phase (park edits target trunk and precede the re-stack edits), park-failure-blocks-push, and the non-reorder no-park path. `syncAllCommand` park coverage.
- **`tests/commands/sync.doc.test.ts`** + **`tests/fixtures/cassettes/commands__sync--070.json`** — a doc/cassette test that reorders a real 2-PR stack against `spry-check` and asserts both PRs stay OPEN. Cassette recorded with `SPRY_RECORD=1` (agent-run).
- **`CHANGELOG.md`** — `[Unreleased]` entry.

Existing helpers reused (no signature changes):

- `expectedBaseFor(unit, units, config): string` from `./stack-analysis.ts`.
- `retargetPR(ctx, prNumber, newBase, { cwd }): Promise<void>` from `../gh/index.ts` (throws on failure).
- `branchForUnit(unit, config): string`, `PRInfo` (`{ number, state, baseRefName, ... }`).

---

---

## Task 9: Manual verification with the `verify` skill

**Files:** none

- [ ] **Step 1: Drive the real flow**

Use the `verify` skill (or the repro pattern from the design phase) to confirm end-to-end against `spry-check`: build a 3-unit stack, open PRs, reorder to (C,A,B), run the real `sp sync`, and confirm all PRs remain OPEN with correct stacked bases. This repeats the design-phase validation against the _shipped_ code path (not a hand-written script).

Expected: all PRs OPEN, bases correctly re-stacked, `spry-check` reset afterward.

- [ ] **Step 2: Final full suite**

Run: `bun test`
Expected: PASS.

---
close_reason: Manual verify: drove shipped sp binary end-to-end against real spry-check with a 3-unit reorder (C,A,B). All 3 PRs OPEN, final bases correctly re-stacked (C->main,A->C,B->A). Park fired only where needed. Full suite 582 pass/4 skip/0 fail. Repo reset clean.

title: Discovery: sync park phase silently skipped if PR lookup throws
description:
In syncCommand (src/commands/sync.ts, Phase-1 park block added in spry-206.13, commit 5e1e3d6), the park is gated on 'if (prMapForPark && stackHasReorder(...))'. If checkSync's findPRsForBranches GraphQL call threw (checked.prMap === undefined), the park phase is skipped ENTIRELY even when a genuine reorder exists — the push then proceeds with unparked stale bases, which can re-introduce the exact spry-206 MERGED bug.

This is NOT a regression from spry-206: the pre-existing post-push retargetMismatched phase has the identical 'prMap ? ... : false' guard, so both phases already no-op on a failed PR lookup. But it means the reorder-merge protection has a hole precisely when GitHub/gh is flaky: a reorder + failed PR lookup + existing branches on remote => push can mark PRs MERGED.

Options to consider: (a) when a reorder is even possible and prMap is unavailable, refuse to push (fail-safe abort) rather than pushing unparked; (b) surface a clear warning that push proceeded without reorder protection. Needs design thought — filed as a discovery, not urgent, since a thrown PR lookup already degrades sync (retarget also skipped) and the reorder+flaky-gh combination is narrow.

title: Discovery: park+push block duplicated between syncCommand and syncAllCommand
description:
The pre-push park logic (stackHasReorder gate → compute existingBranches → parkMismatchedToTrunk → pushExistingBranches with parkFailed skip-set → fold parkFailed into hadFailure) is duplicated in two places in src/commands/sync.ts as of spry-206:
 - syncCommand single-stack path (Phase-1 park block, ~lines 211-241)
 - syncAllCommand per-stack loop (~lines 737-764)

They differ only in loop nesting: syncCommand operates on one flat units[] array; syncAllCommand iterates StackState[]. Extracting a shared 'parkAndPushOneStack(ctx, config, units, existing, prMap, cwd)' helper was judged not worth it for two call sites (would add an abstraction layer for little gain). BUT the two must stay in sync — a future change to the park semantics must touch both. If a THIRD caller ever appears, extract the shared helper then. Low priority; not a bug. Filed per code-review recommendation during spry-206.15.

title: Discovery: consider aborting whole-stack push on any park failure (defensive)
description:
During spry-206 final review, an adversarial pass initially flagged (then withdrew) a concern that the park-failure fail-safe skips the wrong branch: it excludes the FAILED PR's own head branch from the push, but the theoretically-endangering push would be the branch that is that PR's STALE BASE (a different unit).

Verdict after SHA-level analysis + two live repros against spry-check: NOT reachable today, so NOT a bug. Reason (invariant): sp's reorder rewrites every repositioned commit to a fresh SHA via commit-tree (src/git/plumbing.ts rebasePlumbing). GitHub marks MERGED by commit-SHA reachability. A PR is parked only if MISMATCHED (its position changed => its head SHA was rewritten => appears in no other branch). A PR whose OLD head SHA survives verbatim into another pushed branch's tip must be in an unchanged bottom prefix => MATCHED => never parked. The parked set and the surviving-old-SHA set are disjoint, so a park failure can never leave a PR reachable from another pushed branch. The current per-branch skip is therefore safe.

DEFENSIVE HARDENING (optional, low priority): make a park failure abort the ENTIRE stack's push (push nothing) rather than skipping only the failed PR's own branch. This would remove reliance on the SHA-rewriting invariant and be robust if the reorder implementation ever changed to preserve SHAs. Trade-off: aborts more work on a transient gh error. Decide if worth it. Location: src/commands/sync.ts syncCommand Phase-1 park block (~217-241) and syncAllCommand per-stack loop (~737-764). A code comment recording the SHA-reachability rationale was added to parkMismatchedToTrunk in spry-206 to prevent re-deriving this the hard way.

title: Brainstorm non-interactive fallbacks for all TUI commands
description:
Our interactive TUI commands (sp sync --open, sp group, bare sp land) block on stdin and cannot be driven by agents or scripts. Brainstorm non-interactive fallbacks/equivalents for each so automation (agents, CI, scripting) can exercise the full workflow.

Known interactive commands to cover:
- sp sync --open (TUI prompt when opening PRs)
- sp group (interactive grouping/reordering editor)
- sp land (bare, no --through — single-select picker)

Prior art from main (dropped in rebuild, see docs/rebuild-roadmap.md 'sp group helper capabilities — dropped'): sp group had --apply <json> for non-interactive grouping, --fix[=dissolve|merge], and group dissolve with --inherit/--no-inherit. Roadmap says: if these resurface, redesign rebuild-native against refs/spry/groups, do not port main's commands blindly.

Scope of brainstorm: for each interactive command, decide the shape of a non-interactive path (flag-driven? env var? JSON stdin? separate subcommand?), what it unblocks (agent dogfooding, CI, tests), and whether it should be a public feature or a test-only hook. AGENTS.md currently asks the user to run interactive commands manually during dogfooding — a non-interactive fallback would remove that friction.

title: sync: reconcile manual reorders / ID edits / mid-stack membership changes
description:
When a user hand-edits their stack outside spry and then runs `sp sync`, spry must reconcile its own state (Spry-Commit-Id trailers, PR mappings, PR bases, branches) to match the new reality. This bead audits and hardens that reconciliation across the manual-edit scenarios.

GUIDING INVARIANT: identity lives in the Spry-Commit-Id trailer, NOT in the commit SHA. Any operation that rewrites SHAs while preserving trailers (rebase, reorder, reword, squash, split, edit) is THE SAME CASE from sync's point of view: sync must follow ids, not SHAs, end-to-end. Reword/squash/split are not special — they are just SHA-rewriting rebases and should need no bespoke handling once the id-following path is correct. The scenarios below are the distinct behaviors to audit; they are not independent code paths.

SCENARIOS TO COVER:

1) SHA-rewriting edits with trailers intact (rebase / reorder / reword / squash / split / commit --amend).
   - Common case: ids preserved, SHAs change, possibly order changes. Sync must map each surviving id to its existing PR and reconcile stacked-PR bases to the new sequence. Squash MERGES ids (two commits -> one): decide which id survives and what happens to the dropped id's PR (treat as a mid-stack removal, scenario 3). Split SPLITS an id (one commit -> two): the second commit has no id and gets one injected (treat as a mid-stack addition, scenario 3).
   - PARTIALLY DONE for reorder: spry-206 added a reorder-safe pre-push 'park' phase in src/commands/sync.ts (stackHasReorder() ~line 516, parkMismatchedToTrunk(), park block ~line 218, --all loop ~line 748): it parks every open+mismatched PR onto trunk before the force-push so an in-place push can't make a head reachable from a stale base (which GitHub would mark MERGED). VERIFY this fully covers base re-computation for all SHA-rewriting edits, not just the merge-safety park. Related discovery bugs: spry-az6j (park silently skipped if PR lookup throws), spry-8vz2 (abort whole-stack push on any park failure), spry-0xrq (park+push duplicated between syncCommand and syncAllCommand).

2) Hand-edited Spry-Commit-Id trailers (user adds/removes/duplicates/edits ids by hand).
   - injectMissingIds() (src/commands/sync.ts ~line 187, ~695) backfills MISSING ids today. GAP: it does not validate for DUPLICATE/collided ids across commits, or malformed ids. Two commits sharing one id (copy/paste, cherry-pick, or a botched split) corrupt the commit->unit->PR mapping in src/parse/stack.ts (commitId keys groups and unit ids, stack.ts ~line 25,51,75). Decide the policy: detect collisions and re-mint one side, or hard-error pointing at the offending commits. Add validation in src/parse/validation.ts and surface it in sync.

3) Stack membership changes (commits added or removed mid-stack).
   - Added (incl. the new half of a split): id injected, opens a new PR on --open, surrounding PRs retarget bases correctly.
   - Removed (incl. the absorbed half of a squash): leaves an orphaned PR + branch. Sync should recognize the unit is gone and reconcile (close/scrub the PR-cache entry, or at least warn). Confirm current behavior and define the intended one (land already scrubs deterministically; sync's story for a user-dropped commit is unclear).

DELIVERABLES:
- Doc tests in tests/commands/sync.doc.test.ts exercising: reorder, reword, squash, split, duplicate-id, mid-stack insert, mid-stack drop. Source of truth for docs/generated/.
- Any code fixes needed for correct reconciliation (duplicate-id validation for #2, orphan handling for #3). Expect #1 for non-reorder edits to mostly work if the id-following path is sound — the value there is proving it with tests.
- Update docs/rebuild-roadmap.md if this settles a design decision about sync reconciliation.

Files of interest: src/commands/sync.ts, src/parse/stack.ts, src/parse/id.ts, src/parse/validation.ts, src/parse/trailers.ts.
notes:
PROBE RESULTS (2026-07-13, runtime-confirmed via throwaway detectPRUnits harness + code trace):

DUP-ID (confirmed, worse than 'corrupt mapping' — concrete branch collision):
- detectPRUnits on 2 distinct commits sharing id 'dup00001' yields TWO separate single units BOTH with id=dup00001. branchForUnit -> both resolve to the SAME branch prefix/dup00001.
- parseStack returns ok:TRUE. No error, no warning. Silent.
- Runtime consequence: push phase pushes both units to the one branch (last-write-wins clobber -> one commit's content vanishes from its PR); findPRsForBranches + PR-cache key on dup00001 collapse both units to one PR entry.
- CONFIRMED the check must count id-string occurrences across the RAW COMMIT LIST, not per-unit: a legit group collapses 2 DISTINCT ids into 1 unit (commitIds=[id1,id2]) and must NOT be flagged. So: detectDuplicateIds(commits) = any Spry-Commit-Id appearing on >=2 commit objects. Wire before/inside parseStack's ok-return.

MID-STACK DROP (confirmed by code trace, no runtime harness needed):
- findPRsForBranches queries units.map(branchForUnit) (sync.ts:152) — dropped id's branch never queried.
- writePRCache rebuilds from current units (sync.ts:623) and savePRCache replaces whole tree — dropped id's cache entry clobbered out silently.
- Nothing else observes the old cache: checkSync loads it into CheckSyncResult.prCache (sync.ts:166) but syncCommand NEVER reads checked.prCache. => The 'before' snapshot is ALREADY in the return value, unused. D2 sync-side warning can diff checked.prCache vs current unit ids with NO new fetch.
- Confirmed: dropped unit's open PR + remote branch dangle untouched. No orphan detection anywhere in sync. Matches design assumption exactly.

IMPLEMENTATION HOOKS NAILED DOWN:
- D1: detectDuplicateIds(commits: CommitWithTrailers[]) in validation.ts; call it in parseStack (return {ok:false, error:'duplicate-id', ...offenders}) OR in checkSync right after parseStack. parseStack is better — shared by every read path automatically. Add a StackParseResult error variant + formatValidationError case.
- D2: in syncCommand, after checkSync, diff Object.keys(checked.prCache) against current unit ids; warn for each cached id absent from units. Zero new I/O.
- D3: clean.ts new reap path uses tracked-branches/PR-cache ids vs live stacks to find orphan-by-absence; deleteRemoteBranch (auto-closes PR).

title: sp land: stop retargeting PRs to trunk (per-PR diff fidelity)
description:
DESIGN / rationale for the sp land no-retarget fix. NOT yet implemented on main.

RELATED: the step-by-step implementation plan is bead spry-8lz (full verbatim plan doc inlined there).

SOURCE: this content summarizes the design doc that lived at docs/plans/2026-07-06-land-no-retarget-design.md on the local branch fix/land-no-retarget (commit ff949c8). That branch also added an "Outstanding work" bullet to AGENTS.md pointing at the plan docs; both the branch and that AGENTS.md note are superseded by these two beads.

PROBLEM: landCommand retargets every in-scope PR base to config.trunk BEFORE the ff-push (src/commands/land.ts, the 'Retarget every scope PR to trunk BEFORE the push' loop, ~lines 147-161). This corrupts each merged PR's frozen three-dot diff. GitHub marks a PR MERGED on default-branch reachability (which the ff-push already guarantees), NOT on its base — so the retarget is unnecessary for MERGED status. But because it runs while trunk is still at old-trunk, each PR k's base becomes old-trunk and its frozen diff becomes old-trunk..head_k (cumulative: bottom PR shows 1 commit, top PR shows the whole stack + union of files).

FIX: Delete the retarget loop entirely; leave every PR on its stacked base through the push. Remove the retargetPR import from land.ts. KEEP retargetPR in src/gh/pr.ts / src/gh/index.ts — src/commands/sync.ts still uses it legitimately. Everything else unchanged (readiness gate, ff-push, cleanup tail: dropLandedFromPRCache/scrubLandedGroupRecords/deleteSpentBranches, autoDeleteOnLand). After: sp land makes zero 'gh pr edit' calls; only GitHub traffic is the readiness api graphql lookups.

OUT OF SCOPE (decided): no poll-until-MERGED; no delete_branch_on_merge / ruleset toggling; no change to autoDeleteOnLand. Accepted caveat: with autoDeleteOnLand on + 2+ PR stack, deleting a parent head branch before GitHub's async merge-marking can momentarily orphan a child PR (base vanishes -> may flip CLOSED). Accepted because autoDeleteOnLand is opt-in/default off; sp clean is the recovery tool.

TESTS (delete-and-replace in tests/commands/land.test.ts): DELETE test 'retargets every off-trunk scope PR to trunk BEFORE pushing' (~lines 264-322). ADD a replacement asserting a land over a stacked stack makes NO 'gh pr edit' calls while origin/main still advances to the target tip (count pr edit calls, expect zero, via stubGh). Other whole-stack/partial/readiness/cleanup tests are unaffected.

DOC + CASSETTE (must re-record — HUMAN-RUN): tests/commands/land.doc.test.ts fragment order 010 'Landing through a commit'. Rewrite the prose (~line 107) which currently falsely says retargeting is what makes PRs MERGED. New prose: sp land --through <id> lands from the bottom through <id>; fast-forwards trunk to that unit's tip; never uses the GitHub merge API and never retargets PR bases; GitHub marks each PR MERGED via default-branch reachability; leaving each PR on its stacked base keeps its diff scoped to that unit; sp land never deletes branches (sp clean's job). Requires SPRY_RECORD=1 cassette re-record (needs gh auth + HTTPS git) — ask the user to run it.

ALSO UPDATE: docs/rebuild-roadmap.md land decisions + CHANGELOG [Unreleased]. Full task breakdown is in spry-8lz.
close_reason: Land no-retarget fix merged to main (b15ef1b). Core fix, docs, roadmap, changelog all shipped; Task 5 cassette re-record (spry-dzp.5) and blocker spry-xil both CLOSED 2026-07-12. Epic complete.

title: Plan: sp land no-retarget fix — step-by-step implementation
description:
Step-by-step implementation plan for the sp land no-retarget fix. This is the verbatim content of the plan doc that lived at docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-06-land-no-retarget.md on the local branch fix/land-no-retarget (commit ff949c8), preserved here because beads is the only record. The design/rationale is the related bead spry-4k6. Task 5 (re-record land cassettes) is a HUMAN-RUN step: needs gh auth + HTTPS git config. NOT yet implemented on main.

# `sp land` — Stop Retargeting PRs to Trunk — Implementation Plan

> **For agentic workers:** REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers:subagent-driven-development (recommended) or superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan task-by-task. Steps use checkbox (`- [ ]`) syntax for tracking.

**Goal:** Remove `sp land`'s pre-push "retarget every PR to trunk" step so each merged PR keeps its own single-unit diff instead of a cumulative one.

**Architecture:** `sp land` currently retargets every in-scope PR's base to trunk _before_ the fast-forward push, which freezes a cumulative `old-trunk…head_k` diff into each merged PR. GitHub marks a PR `MERGED` on reachability from the **default branch**, not on its base — so the ff-push alone already yields `MERGED` status. We delete the retarget loop; land becomes a pure `git push` (plus readiness lookups and the existing cleanup tail). PRs keep their stacked bases, so each frozen diff stays scoped to its unit.

**Tech Stack:** TypeScript, Bun (`bun test`), git plumbing, `gh` CLI via a cassette record/replay seam.

**Design doc:** [`docs/plans/2026-07-06-land-no-retarget-design.md`](../../plans/2026-07-06-land-no-retarget-design.md)

**Environment note:** This machine has git 2.52.0 (≥ 2.40), so plain `bun test` / `bun run` are correct — no `:docker` alias needed. If executed on a machine with git < 2.40, substitute the `:docker` aliases per `AGENTS.md`.

---

## File Structure

| File                                                | Change            | Responsibility                                                                                    |
| --------------------------------------------------- | ----------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `src/commands/land.ts`                              | Modify            | Remove the retarget loop and the `retargetPR` import. Land becomes ff-push + readiness + cleanup. |
| `tests/commands/land.test.ts`                       | Modify            | Delete the retarget-ordering test; add a "makes no `gh pr edit` calls" test.                      |
| `tests/commands/land.doc.test.ts`                   | Modify            | Rewrite fragment-010 prose; remove the dead `retargeted PR #…` scrub.                             |
| `tests/fixtures/cassettes/commands__land--010.json` | Re-record (human) | Faithful recording of the new no-retarget `gh` traffic.                                           |
| `tests/fixtures/cassettes/commands__land--020.json` | Re-record (human) | Same, via the interactive picker path.                                                            |
| `docs/generated/commands/land.{md,html}`            | Regenerate        | Reflect new prose; no "retargeted PR #…" line.                                                    |
| `docs/rebuild-roadmap.md`                           | Modify            | Correct the `sp land` decision text.                                                              |
| `CHANGELOG.md`                                      | Modify            | Add a `Fixed` entry; correct the two stale `Added` entries.                                       |

`retargetPR` **stays** in `src/gh/pr.ts` and `src/gh/index.ts` — `src/commands/sync.ts:384` still uses it. Only land's _use_ of it is removed.

---

## Task 1: Add the failing "no retarget" test (TDD red)

**Files:**

- Test: `tests/commands/land.test.ts` (add a test inside the existing `describe("sp land --through", ...)` block)

The existing harness (`stubGh`, `ghPrStub`, `publishedStack`, `runLand`, `captureLogs`, `trapExit`, `makeConfiguredRepo`, `makeCtx`) is already defined at the top of this file — reuse it. `stubGh` returns `{ gh, calls }`; each recorded call is `{ args: string[]; stdin?: string }`. A `gh pr edit` call has `args[0] === "pr" && args[1] === "edit"`.

- [ ] **Step 1: Write the failing test**

Add this test immediately after the existing `"--through the first unit lands only the bottom…"` test (i.e. before the current `"retargets every off-trunk scope PR to trunk BEFORE pushing"` test, which Task 2 deletes):

```ts
  test("makes no `gh pr edit` calls: PRs keep their stacked bases, trunk still advances", async () => {
    const repo = await makeConfiguredRepo();
    const git = createRealGitRunner();
    await publishedStack(repo, git, [
      { id: "aaa11111", subject: "first" },
      { id: "bbb22222", subject: "second" },
    ]);
    const tip = (await git.run(["rev-parse", "HEAD"], { cwd: repo.path })).stdout.trim();

    // The upper PR (#2) is stacked on the lower unit's branch. Under the old
    // flow land would retarget it to `main` before pushing. It must not anymore.
    const { gh, calls } = stubGh(
      ghPrStub({
        "spry/test/aaa11111": { number: 1, base: "main" },
        "spry/test/bbb22222": { number: 2, base: "spry/test/aaa11111" },
      }),
    );
    const ctx = makeCtx(repo, gh);
    const logs = captureLogs();
    const trap = trapExit();
    try {
      await runLand(ctx, { cwd: repo.path, through: "bbb22222" });
    } finally {
      trap.restore();
      logs.restore();
    }

    // No PR was retargeted — land emits zero `gh pr edit` calls.
    const editCalls = calls.filter((c) => c.args[0] === "pr" && c.args[1] === "edit");
    expect(editCalls).toHaveLength(0);
    // MERGED-by-reachability still holds: origin/main advanced to the tip.
    const originMain = (
      await git.run(["rev-parse", "origin/main"], { cwd: repo.path })
    ).stdout.trim();
    expect(originMain).toBe(tip);
    expect(trap.exitCode).toBeUndefined();
  });
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Run the test to verify it FAILS**

Run: `bun test tests/commands/land.test.ts -t "makes no .gh pr edit. calls"`

Expected: FAIL. `editCalls` will have length 1 (the current code retargets PR #2 to `main`), so `expect(editCalls).toHaveLength(0)` fails.

- [ ] **Step 3: Commit the red test**

```bash
git add tests/commands/land.test.ts
git commit -m "test(land): assert land makes no gh pr edit calls (red)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>"
```

---

## Task 2: Remove the retarget loop (TDD green)

**Files:**

- Modify: `src/commands/land.ts` (remove import of `retargetPR`; remove the retarget loop, current lines 147–161)

- [ ] **Step 1: Remove `retargetPR` from the import block**

In `src/commands/land.ts`, the import from `../gh/index.ts` currently reads:

```ts
import {
  findPRsForBranches,
  retargetPR,
  pushBranch,
  deleteRemoteBranch,
  isAlreadyGone,
} from "../gh/index.ts";
```

Change it to (drop the `retargetPR` line):

```ts
import {
  findPRsForBranches,
  pushBranch,
  deleteRemoteBranch,
  isAlreadyGone,
} from "../gh/index.ts";
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Delete the retarget loop**

Remove this entire block (currently step 5, between the unresolved-review-threads block and the ff-push `// 6. One ff push…`):

```ts
  // 5. Retarget every scope PR to trunk BEFORE the push (the secret sauce).
  for (const unit of scopeUnits) {
    const branch = branchForUnit(unit, config);
    const pr = prMap.get(branch);
    if (!pr || pr.state !== "OPEN") continue;
    if (pr.baseRefName === config.trunk) continue;
    try {
      await retargetPR(ctx, pr.number, config.trunk, { cwd });
      console.log(`↻ retargeted PR #${pr.number} → ${config.trunk}`);
    } catch (err) {
      const message = err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err);
      console.error(`✗ Could not retarget PR #${pr.number}: ${message}`);
      process.exit(1); // never ff with an un-retargeted middle PR
    }
  }

```

The comment on the next block currently says `// 6. One ff push to the target tip.` — renumber it to `// 5. One ff push to the target tip.` for tidiness (optional but keeps the numbered comments contiguous; the following cleanup comment `// 7.` → `// 6.` and `// 8.` → `// 7.` likewise). This is comment-only.

- [ ] **Step 3: Run the new test to verify it PASSES**

Run: `bun test tests/commands/land.test.ts -t "makes no .gh pr edit. calls"`

Expected: PASS. Land no longer calls `gh pr edit`; `origin/main` still advances to the tip via the ff-push.

- [ ] **Step 4: Verify no unused-import / type errors**

```markdown
- **`sp land` was redesigned, not ported.** `main`'s land merged PRs via the
  GitHub merge API (`--all` merged the stack bottom-up, waiting on each merge).
  This branch instead does a single fast-forward push of trunk to the target

  before the ff-push, on the theory that a PR is marked `MERGED` only when its
  head lands in its own base. That theory was wrong in a way that corrupted the
  record: GitHub marks a PR `MERGED` on reachability from the **default branch**,
  not its base, so the ff-push alone suffices — and retargeting while trunk was
  still at its old position froze a cumulative `old-trunk…head_k` three-dot diff
  into each merged PR (bottom PR = 1 commit, top PR = the whole stack). Leaving
  each PR on its stacked base keeps every PR's diff scoped to its own unit. See
  `docs/plans/2026-07-06-land-no-retarget-design.md`.
```

STATUS 2026-07-12: MERGED TO MAIN (merge commit b15ef1b, pushed). The core fix shipped: sp land no longer retargets in-scope PRs to trunk; it fast-forwards trunk to the target tip and leaves each PR on its stacked base, so each merged PR keeps its own single-unit diff (GitHub marks MERGED via default-branch reachability). Land makes zero gh pr edit calls.
