title: Discovery: land cassette recording pollutes spry-check main; fixture reset does not restore baseline
description: While re-recording the sp land cassettes (spry-dzp.5 / spry-8lz Task 5), the record run failed with 'No commit or group matching bbb22222 found in stack' and the embedded sp sync only pushed one branch. ROOT CAUSE: spry-check's main was left pointing at a landed commit 'Add login / Spry-Commit-Id: aaa11111' from a PRIOR land recording. sp land's whole job is to fast-forward main to a stack tip, so recording it MUTATES spry-check's main. createGitHubFixture().reset() (used by the doc test) evidently does NOT roll main back to the pristine single-commit baseline that scripts/setup-spry-check.ts establishes. On the next record run, because main already contains aaa11111, the stack parse no longer sees a clean 2-commit stack and land aborts. WORKAROUND used this session: re-run 'SPRY_TEST_REPO_SETUP_SKIP_CONFIRM=1 bun run scripts/setup-spry-check.ts' to reset main to one commit before each land re-record. FIX TO CONSIDER: make the land doc-test fixture reset restore main to the baseline commit (or have the land fragments record against a throwaway ref / reset main in setup), so land cassettes are re-recordable without a manual setup-script run. Files: tests/commands/land.doc.test.ts (setupLandStack + fixture.reset), tests/lib/ github fixture (createGitHubFixture), scripts/setup-spry-check.ts. Relates to epic spry-dzp.
close_reason: Fixed. createGitHubFixture().reset() now calls restoreMainToBaseline(): finds the default branch's root commit (the single commit setup-spry-check.ts force-pushes) and force-PATCHes the branch ref back to it when ahead, reporting movement as CleanupReport.mainRestored. This closes the 2nd record-mode pollution vector (distinct from spry-xil's stale refs/spry/* purge): sp land fast-forwards origin/main past baseline and branch cleanup skips the default branch, so main stayed advanced and the next record run branched feature/x off the already-landed main, corrupting the stack parse. TDD: red test 'reset restores main to the single-commit baseline' in tests/lib/github-fixture.test.ts (SPRY_RECORD-gated like siblings), seeds an advance then asserts reset() returns main to 1 commit. VERIFIED END-TO-END: with the fix, both land cassettes re-recorded back-to-back (SPRY_RECORD=1, fragments 010+020) with ZERO manual setup-spry-check.ts runs — the exact scenario the bead documented as broken. spry-check main confirmed at 1 commit after fixture-test runs (was 6, polluted). Full offline suite 582 pass/0 fail/5 skip; all 5 SPRY_RECORD fixture tests pass. Files: tests/lib/github-fixture.ts (restoreMainToBaseline + reset wiring + CleanupReport.mainRestored), tests/lib/github-fixture.test.ts (red->green test + bumped 'reset leaves zero open PRs' to 60s since reset now makes more gh calls). Cassette re-record intentionally reverted (noise-only PR-number/cwd churn, scrubbed in docs). Changelog updated.

title: Doc-test record mode races on shared spry-check repo (needs serialization)
description:
ROOT CAUSE PROVEN. The three doc-test files that use a live GitHub fixture — tests/commands/sync.doc.test.ts, land.doc.test.ts, group.doc.test.ts — all mutate the SINGLE shared happycollision/spry-check repo, and there is ZERO serialization. Bun runs test files concurrently across threads, so under 'SPRY_RECORD=1 bun test' (full suite) they race: every doc test calls fixture.reset() (createGitHubFixture in tests/lib/github-fixture.ts), which is REPO-WIDE — closeAllPRs + deleteAllBranches + restoreMainToBaseline + purgeSpryRefs. While test A is mid-flight (branch pushed, waiting on CI), test B's reset() deletes A's branch/PR, so A fails with 'GraphQL: No commits between main and <branch>', a CLOSED PR, or a CI-wait timeout.

EVIDENCE (decisive): a full-suite record run fails a SHIFTING subset of these tests between identical runs (run 1: sync order 50+60; run 2 after fixing those: sync order 25+60, with 60 a 60s timeout) — the signature of a race, not a deterministic bug. Recording each live-fixture file SERIALLY (one 'bun test <file>' at a time) passes ALL of them: sync 8/8, land 2/2, group 6/6. So every individual test is correct; only concurrent execution against the shared repo fails. This has never worked concurrently — every committed cassette was recorded one file/fragment at a time; nobody had run a full-suite record until now.

FIX (decided): add a SPRY_RECORD-gated cross-process advisory filesystem lock (e.g. a lockfile under .test-tmp) that each live-fixture doc test acquires for its ENTIRE body — setup -> sp invocation -> assertions -> final reset — and releases at the end. Serialize ONLY under record mode; offline replay must stay fully parallel/fast (no lock). Granularity matters: a lock held only during reset() does NOT help, because tests interleave between reset and their PR work — the whole test body is the critical section.

AFFECTED: tests/lib/github-fixture.ts (createGitHubFixture / reset), tests/commands/{sync,land,group}.doc.test.ts (each fixture-using docTest body). Consider a helper in tests/lib that wraps 'acquire lock -> run -> release' so all three files use it uniformly.

CONTEXT: surfaced while fulfilling a request to run the full suite in record mode twice and prove docs don't churn. Related to spry-bei (fixture reset baseline restore, fixed) and spry-xil (stale refs/spry purge, fixed) — same theme: record-mode fixture hygiene. Also relates to spry-svu (5s-default-timeout flake on github-fixture.test.ts network tests, seen again this run).
close_reason: Fixed: added SPRY_RECORD-gated cross-process advisory lock. New tests/lib/record-lock.ts (atomic mkdir + stale-lock recovery, 6 unit tests) and withGitHubFixture() wrapper in tests/lib/github-fixture.ts (3 unit tests) serialize the entire body of every live-fixture doc test (sync/land/group, 8 bodies) under record mode; replay stays fully parallel (fixture=undefined, no lock). PROOF: full-suite 'SPRY_RECORD=1 bun test' now passes 595/595 (previously failed a shifting subset — the race signature); playback builds are byte-identical (docs deterministic). Offline suite 590 pass/0 fail; bun run check clean. Discovered a pre-existing, independent order-20 doc-scrub non-determinism (missing PR-number + record-mode origin-URL scrubs) -> filed spry-w313; docs/generated restored to HEAD, not committed here.

title: Discovery: sync.doc order-20 fragment non-deterministic on re-record (missing PR-number + origin-URL scrubs)
description:
Found while proving spry-cteo (full-suite record serialization). A full-suite SPRY_RECORD=1 run regenerated docs/generated/commands/sync.{md,html} with unstable content for the 'Opening a new PR' fragment (tests/commands/sync.doc.test.ts, order 20):

1) PR NUMBER not scrubbed: the fragment shows the literal GitHub-minted PR number ('Created PR #1148' / 'pull/1148'), which changes on every re-record. The committed doc had '#1001'. Sibling tests (orders 22, 25, 70) all scrub with doc.scrub(/Created PR #\d+/g, 'Created PR #42') and doc.scrub(/pull\/\d+/g, 'pull/42'); order 20 is MISSING both scrubs.

2) ORIGIN-URL scrub ineffective in record mode + md/html divergence: order 20 does doc.scrub(/https:\/\/github\.com\/[^/]+\/spry-check/g, 'https://github.com/owner/repo'). But doc.scrub(repo) (tests/lib/doc.ts:90-91) also substitutes repo.originPath -> '/tmp/repo-origin'. In RECORD mode originPath = fixture.repoUrl = https://github.com/happycollision/spry-check, so the PR URL prefix gets rewritten to '/tmp/repo-origin/pull/1148' in the .md, while the .html rendered 'https://github.com/owner/repo/pull/1148' — the two generated formats DISAGREE for the same fragment. In REPLAY the PR URL comes from the cassette (a real github URL) and originPath is a local /tmp bare path, so the github-host scrub fires and both formats agree — which is why this only surfaces on a fresh record.

FIX SKETCH: give order 20 the same PR-number scrubs as its siblings, and make the origin/URL canonicalization record-safe (e.g. scrub the fixture repo URL to owner/repo BEFORE doc.scrub(repo) rewrites originPath, or have doc.scrub(repo) use a github.com-neutral replacement). Verify md and html match after a record.

IMPACT: pre-existing (independent of the spry-cteo race fix); the committed docs were already stale (source uses Spry-Commit-Id aaa11111 but committed sync.md shows aaaa1111). Blocks a clean pre-merge record+playback gate for any branch that re-records the sync cassettes. Not fixing here to avoid derailing spry-cteo.
close_reason: Fixed: order-20 sync.doc fragment now gets PR-number scrubs and canonicalizes the github host before doc.scrub(repo) so record mode is deterministic; same scrub-ordering fix applied to sibling fragments 22/25/60/70. Verified via full-suite record + double replay: only intended #1001->#42 churn in docs/generated. The 'aaaa1111' concern in the issue was a misread — that is the doc builder's deterministic spry-id placeholder, not stale.

title: Discovery: github-fixture.test.ts flaky under SPRY_RECORD (read-after-write eventual consistency)
description:
Found while running the full pre-merge record gate for spry-w313 (SPRY_RECORD=1 bun test). tests/lib/github-fixture.test.ts fails intermittently in record mode; it blocks a clean full-suite record gate.

OBSERVED (two record runs):
- Run A (full suite): 1 fail — 'reset restores main to the single-commit baseline' (line 140): expect(mainCommitCount).toBe(1) got 2.
- Run B (isolated: bun test tests/lib/github-fixture.test.ts, clean repo, no concurrent mutators): 2 fail — 'reset leaves zero open PRs' (line 62) with 'Failed to purge spry refs: Refusing to mutate ... missing safety marker' AND 'reset restores main to the single-commit baseline' (line 140) mainCommitCount 2 != 1.
- The set of failing tests SHIFTS between runs (the eventual-consistency tell). After every run the LIVE repo is clean: main=1 commit, README has marker, 0 open PRs, 0 spry refs — so there is NO persistent repo corruption; reset() DOES eventually restore. The failures are read-after-write races in the ASSERTIONS.

ROOT CAUSE: GitHub's Contents/git-refs/commits APIs are eventually consistent. The file already acknowledges this for refs (waitForSpryRef polls). But:
1) mainCommitCount(owner,repo) (line 27) does a single 'gh api commits?sha=main --jq length' read with no polling; called immediately after reset()->restoreMainToBaseline() PATCHes main back to root (github-fixture.ts:280). The read can return the stale pre-PATCH listing (2 commits) before replicas catch up -> expect(...).toBe(1) fails spuriously.
2) verifyTestRepo (github-fixture.ts:60) reads contents/README.md on the default branch; during/after a main rewrite a stale/empty read can briefly miss the safety marker, so assertSafeToMutate throws inside purgeSpryRefs -> report.errors non-empty (line 62/91).
3) github-fixture.test.ts gates on SPRY_RECORD (line 40) but does NOT hold the record-lock that withGitHubFixture uses for the doc tests, so under a full-suite record its reset() calls run concurrently with locked doc-test bodies mutating the same shared spry-check main — amplifying (1)/(2).

NOT caused by spry-w313 (that change is doc-scrub only: sync.doc.test.ts + docs/generated + CHANGELOG; does not touch tests/lib/github-fixture*). Introduced with the spry-bei restoreMainToBaseline work (commit 92e4577) + spry-cteo (f3edf9d).

FIX SKETCH: give the assertions the same eventual-consistency tolerance as waitForSpryRef — poll mainCommitCount until it reaches the expected value (with timeout) rather than a single read; likewise retry verifyTestRepo/assertSafeToMutate briefly. Optionally run github-fixture.test.ts under the record-lock (or serialize it) so it does not race concurrent doc-test main rewrites. IMPACT: blocks the AGENTS.md pre-merge 'record whole suite once' gate for ANY branch, because a full SPRY_RECORD=1 bun test flakily fails on this file even when the actual fixture machinery works.
close_reason: Fixed via condition-based waiting (tests/lib/wait-for.ts waitForValue) + shared record-lock. Root causes: (1) mainCommitCount single read asserted immediately after reset()'s ref PATCH raced the eventually-consistent commits endpoint; (2) verifyTestRepo single README read transiently missed the safety marker during concurrent main rewrites; (3) the file ran unlocked during full-suite record, so its reset()/main rewrites raced concurrent doc-test sp land. Fix polls both assertion sites, retries only inconclusive marker reads (preserving the safety guard), and wraps the three mutating tests in withSpryCheckRecordLock. VERIFIED: full AGENTS.md pre-merge record+double-replay gate run TWICE end-to-end (SPRY_RECORD=1 bun test = 598 tests 0 fail each time; docs/generated churn = <none> both passes; only cassette noise, dropped). Also github-fixture.test.ts green 3x consecutively in isolation. Commit 187fb31.

title: Simplify doc-test determinism machinery and unlock parallelism
description:
Findings from a full audit of the testing/doc-generation machinery (2026-07-13 session).

KEY INSIGHT: determinism already lives at the OUTPUT boundary — the scrub pools (tests/lib/sha-scanner.ts) and args-keyed cassettes (src/lib/replaying-client.ts, match: args) — not at the input. Byte-stable git SHAs are NOT load-bearing anywhere: (1) no committed cassette contains a 40-hex SHA (all gh calls keyed by branch name / graphql args / stdin); (2) docs scrub SHAs via pool mapping, which needs stable discovery ORDER+COUNT from the git log --all --reflog walk, not stable values; (3) no doc test asserts a literal SHA; (4) record mode already produces different SHAs than replay (record clones spry-check, replay uses a local bare origin with a different root commit) and land.doc.test.ts already injects a per-run nonce.

MEASUREMENTS: offline replay suite = 166s sequential at 71% of one CPU (subprocess-bound). bun test --concurrent = 63s (2.6x) but 149 failures, all one mechanical class: shared cleanup arrays (repoManager afterEach + doc-test repos[]/afterAll) delete other tests repo dirs mid-run -> ENOENT posix_spawn git. Record mode is dominated by real CI waits in land tests (up to 240s per PR), fully serialized today by the global record lock + per-test double reset.

TARGET END STATE: one determinism story (pools at output boundary; per-run seeded monotonic commit dates for walk order + per-run SHA uniqueness); TUI doc tests wait for process EXIT not sentinel text; per-test cleanup scoping so replay runs --concurrent; cassettes byte-stable across re-recordings (zero-churn pre-merge gate); per-test unit IDs + per-test trunk branches so record mode parallelizes with CI waits overlapping (wall clock = max not sum), with ONE canonical land test kept on real main for MERGED-via-default-branch fidelity (see beads spry-4k6).

Steps are chained children of this epic; br ready surfaces them one at a time in order. Reference: docs/investigations/2026-07-07-group-reflog-nondeterminism.md.
close_reason: All 7 steps delivered and gate-proven: TUI tests end on exit; replay concurrent (~55s vs 166s); record concurrent (~140s vs ~320s, wall clock = max of CI waits); one safety check + single leading reset per fixture; cassettes normalized (residual churn = spry-76lu mid-flight class only); per-run seeded dates; setupDocRepo; per-test trunks/prefixes + canonical land test; scrub-walk race fixed (spry-4zs6). Final commit 5a63a4f.

title: terminal-driver: add waitForExit + graceful close; end TUI doc tests on exit, not sentinel+kill
description:
Step 1 of epic spry-qfc8. Eliminates the kill-timing race class proven in docs/investigations/2026-07-07-group-reflog-nondeterminism.md (waitForText(Reordered) + close() killed the harness mid-write, flipping the scrubber reflog SHA count 8<->9 and churning docs).

CHANGES:
- tests/lib/terminal-driver.ts: add waitForExit(opts?: {timeout?}): Promise<number> that awaits proc.exited and returns the exit code (throw with screen dump on timeout). Make close() graceful: close the pty, then race proc.exited against a short grace window (~2s) before proc.kill(); always await proc.exited.
- Screen buffer must remain capturable after exit (it already is — data callback stops, buffer persists). Add a unit test in tests/lib/terminal-driver.test.ts: spawn a short-lived process, waitForExit resolves with code, capture() still returns the rendered text.
- Update TUI doc tests to wait for EXIT at end-of-test instead of a sentinel + kill, keeping waitForText only for genuinely mid-session captures (menus, MOVE MODE, RENAME MODE):
  - tests/commands/group.doc.test.ts: all tests currently end with waitForText(Groups updated|Cancelled) then close() — replace the final wait with waitForExit (harness exits when groupCommand returns).
  - tests/commands/sync.doc.test.ts order 25: waitForText(Sync complete) then capture — capture needs the screen, so waitForExit first, then capture (sync-tui-harness exits after syncCommand+flush).
  - tests/commands/land.doc.test.ts order 20: waitForText(Landed) — same treatment.
- Existing expects stay unchanged (they assert on captured snapshot text).

VERIFY: bun test tests/commands/group.doc.test.ts in a 20x loop produces a constant shas.length in .test-tmp/doc-fragments/commands__group--020.json (the investigation repro). Full replay suite green.
close_reason: waitForExit + graceful close landed (fb33c79, 7272f67); TUI doc tests end on exit; shas.length constant across 20 runs

title: Concurrency-safe test cleanup; make replay suite green under bun test --concurrent
description:
Step 2 of epic spry-qfc8. Measured: bun test = 166s at 71% of one CPU; bun test --concurrent = 63s but 149 failures.

ROOT CAUSE (verified by running it): cleanup is scoped to the FILE, not the test. tests/lib/repo-manager.ts afterEach wipes ALL activeRepos when ANY test in the file finishes; doc-test files use a shared repos[] array cleaned in afterAll. Under --concurrent a finishing test deletes the temp repo dir out from under still-running tests -> Bun.spawn with dead cwd fails as ENOENT posix_spawn git (seen in tests/commands/sync.test.ts, land.test.ts, etc).

CHANGES:
- Scope repo cleanup per test: each test cleans up only the repos it created (try/finally in the test body, or a per-test tracking scheme — repoManager can key repos by the currently running test). Same for the doc-test repos[] arrays in tests/commands/*.doc.test.ts (they also push PTY-driver close wrappers — those too).
- Audit remaining shared mutable state: the module-level rng in tests/lib/unique-id.ts is seeded by tests in tests/lib/repo.test.ts and unique-id.test.ts — make those tests not race (pass an rng locally, or mark that file serial). pathCounter in tests/lib/repo.ts is fine (single process, monotonic).
- Fixed Bun.sleep pacing in TUI tests may need headroom under load — bump only if flaky in practice.
- Decide default: either make the package.json test script use --concurrent once green, or document it as the fast path and keep plain bun test for CI. Note record mode: --concurrent is a prerequisite for step 7 (parallel record), but record stays serialized until then via the existing record lock (withRecordLock is fs-based and works in-process).

VERIFY: bun test --concurrent fully green, 3 consecutive runs; wall clock ~60-70s vs 166s. Doc-stability check: two consecutive concurrent replay runs + bun run docs:build produce identical docs/generated/.
close_reason: Concurrent replay suite green (~55s vs 166s); per-file cleanup scoping, capture mutex, serialChain workaround (Bun bug filed as spry-ojjj); test:concurrent script added

title: Record-mode reset diet: one safety check per fixture, single reset per test
description:
Step 3 of epic spry-qfc8. Record mode currently pays ~2 resets per fixture test and a safety-marker README read (with up-to-10-attempt waitForValue retry loop) on EVERY destructive call — 5+ guarded reads per reset. The eventual-consistency flakiness that waitForValue papers over is largely self-inflicted by re-reading the README right after reset moved main.

CHANGES in tests/lib/github-fixture.ts:
- assertSafeToMutate: verify the safety marker ONCE in createGitHubFixture (it already does) and cache the verdict; destructive methods assert the cached flag instead of re-reading the README. Owner/repo strings are fixed at construction, so per-call re-reads add no real protection. Keep verifyTestRepo + waitForValue for the constructor-time check only.
- withGitHubFixture: drop the trailing reset() in finally — the next record-mode test resets before its body anyway, and the next recording SESSION starts with a reset. Leaves the shared repo dirty after the last test; document that in the jsdoc and tests/fixtures/cassettes/README.md. (Alternative if dirty-at-end is unacceptable: keep a single suite-end reset — but per-test trailing resets go away either way.)
- Update tests/lib/github-fixture.test.ts + github-fixture-wrapper.test.ts expectations (wrapper test asserts the reset-run-reset sequence today).

VERIFY: SPRY_RECORD=1 bun test passes end-to-end; count of gh api calls per fixture test visibly drops; no new flakiness across 2 record runs.
close_reason: Constructor-only safety check with cached verdict; single leading reset per test; ~31->~12 gh calls/test; two green record runs (47e1e55, 18870c4)

title: Cassette hygiene: strip non-stdin options, normalize PR numbers at record time -> zero-churn gate
description:
Step 4 of epic spry-qfc8. Goal: re-recording produces byte-identical cassettes, so the pre-merge gate becomes EXPECT CLEAN GIT STATUS, full stop — no more expected-churn-inspect-and-drop step.

CHURN SOURCES (verified in committed cassettes): (1) options.cwd temp paths (/tmp/spry-test-<pid>-<n>) recorded verbatim in commands__land--020.json and commands__sync--025.json — replay (match: args) only ever reads options.stdin, so cwd is pure noise; (2) GitHub-minted PR numbers (pull/1001..1123) in recorded stdout.

CHANGES:
- src/lib/recording-client.ts: when persisting an entry, keep only options.stdin (drop cwd and anything else). Replay compatibility is free — createReplayingClient matches on args + stdin only.
- PR-number normalization at record time (in recording-client flush or a post-write pass in writeCassette): build a consistent map first-seen-real-number -> 1001, 1002, ... and rewrite ONLY via anchored patterns across every entry stdout: pull/<n> in URLs and "number":<n> in JSON. Consistency across entries is required (the CLI parses the create URL and later matches the graphql number). Do NOT do a bare numeric replace — fields like totalCount collide.
- Unit tests: tests/lib/recording-client.test.ts — record two calls whose stdout share a PR number, assert both rewritten to the same normalized number; assert options.cwd absent from the written cassette; assert replay of the normalized cassette still matches.
- Re-record all cassettes once so committed ones are clean; then update AGENTS.md pre-merge gate section (Expected churn: cassettes only -> Expected churn: NONE; any git diff after record + double playback is a failure) and tests/fixtures/cassettes/README.md.

VERIFY: run the full gate (docs:clean, SPRY_RECORD=1 bun test, docs:build, then bun test + docs:build twice) — git status completely clean at the end. Run recording twice back-to-back: cassettes byte-identical.
close_reason: stdin-only persistence + anchored PR-number normalization (args+stdin+stderr); cassettes re-recorded canonical; gate updated honestly (residual CI check-run churn filed as spry-76lu). Commits 8df39f6, 1ed8f9d

title: Per-run seeded commit dates replace byte-stable SHAs; delete land nonce and false comments
description:
Step 5 of epic spry-qfc8. Byte-stable SHAs are not load-bearing (see epic). What IS load-bearing: stable discovery ORDER+COUNT in the doc scrubber reflog walk (tests/lib/doc.ts collects, sha-scanner pool maps first-seen -> pool slot). Distinct monotonically increasing dates give the git log --all --reflog walk a TOTAL order — stronger than today's all-identical-2020-01-01 dates — while making every run SHAs unique, which GitHub requires (check runs accumulate on repeated SHAs across recording sessions; that is the land readiness-gate trap).

CHANGES:
- tests/lib/repo.ts: replace pinned GIT_AUTHOR_DATE/GIT_COMMITTER_DATE 2020-01-01 with a per-RUN base date (derived once at process start, e.g. from epoch) bumped +1 hour per commit (a counter in the repo-bound git runner and commit/commitFiles helpers). Keep pinned author/committer NAME/EMAIL. Dates must be monotonic per repo; per-run uniqueness is the point — do NOT make them stable across runs.
- tests/commands/land.doc.test.ts: delete the nonce block in setupLandStack (the test-nonce body paragraph, ~lines 49-68) — per-run dates now guarantee unique SHAs. Keep the waitForChecks logic.
- Fix false comments claiming byte-stable SHAs are needed for cassette replay: tests/lib/repo.ts (header comment on DETERMINISTIC_GIT_ENV), tests/commands/sync.doc.test.ts (Deterministic commits ... byte-identical between recording and replay), anywhere else grep finds.
- tests/lib/repo.test.ts: remove/rework the sha-stability meta-tests (seeded repos produce identical SHAs) — the property is intentionally gone. seedUniqueId itself stays (it serves branch-name uniqueness, unrelated).
- Note: TUI harnesses use createRealGitRunner without the env, so bookkeeping commits (refs/spry/groups, tracked-branches) keep wall-clock dates. They sort newest-first deterministically (later than all seeded dates) and the pool covers their values — acceptable; optionally thread the env through the harness spawn for uniformity.

VERIFY: the full pre-merge gate — record once, playback twice, docs/generated byte-identical (this is the empirical proof walk order survived the date-scheme swap). Also 10x replay loop on group.doc.test.ts: constant shas.length and stable generated docs.
close_reason: Per-run seeded monotonic dates (process-global counter, evidence-forced); land nonce deleted; false comments fixed; docs re-baseline proven deterministic via full gate; check-run accumulation half of spry-76lu resolved. Commits acbf5e0, ab05df3, e7741f8

title: setupDocRepo helper: kill the repeated doc-test boilerplate and the scrub-order footgun
description:
Step 6 of epic spry-qfc8. Five fixture doc tests repeat the same ~15-line block, each with a 4-line comment warning that the spry-check URL scrub MUST be registered before doc.scrub(repo) (record mode: repo.originPath IS the fixture URL; wrong order rewrites it to /tmp/repo-origin and leaks a divergent PR URL into docs). A helper that registers scrubs in the right order deletes the footgun instead of documenting it 5 times.

CHANGES:
- New tests/lib/doc-repo.ts (export via tests/lib/index.ts): setupDocRepo(doc, {recording, section, order}) that (1) createRepo({origin: recording ? github : local}), (2) registers scrubs IN ORDER: github-host canonicalization regex first, then doc.scrub(repo), then the PR-number canonicalizations (Created PR #\d+ -> #42, pull/\d+ -> pull/42, parked/retargeted PR #\d+), (3) sets spry.trunk/remote/branchPrefix and pins spry.repo to the SPRY_TEST_REPO_OWNER/NAME slug (default happycollision/spry-check), (4) returns {repo, repoSlug, env: cassetteEnv({section, order})}.
- Replace the blocks in tests/commands/sync.doc.test.ts (orders 20, 22, 25, 60, 70), group.doc.test.ts (order 25), land.doc.test.ts (setupLandStack callers) — the offline-only doc tests (no cassette) can use it too with recording:false semantics or keep their simpler setup.
- Keep per-test additions (extra scrubs, group records, tracked branches) in the test bodies — the helper covers only the invariant prefix.
- Unit test: fragment produced via the helper in fake-record mode (fixture factory overridden) canonicalizes a spry-check URL correctly (regression for the ordering bug).

VERIFY: replay suite green; docs/generated unchanged vs before the refactor (pure refactor — byte-identical docs is the acceptance bar).
close_reason: setupDocRepo helper landed (b86297f); 7 call sites converted; ordering footgun deleted (mutation-tested regression test); docs byte-identical through two playback cycles. Two review findings carried into spry-qfc8.7: recording flag should control returned env; host-scrub regex hardcodes spry-check

title: Per-test trunks + unit IDs: suite-start-only reset, narrow lock, parallel record mode
description:
Step 7 of epic spry-qfc8 — the end-to-end parallelism payoff. After steps 2/5/6: give every fixture doc test its own namespace on spry-check so record-mode tests are mutually independent, reset once at suite start, and run SPRY_RECORD=1 bun test --concurrent so the land CI waits overlap (record wall clock = max of waits, not sum).

DESIGN:
- Per-test trunk: each fixture test pushes trunk/<section>-<order> from the spry-check baseline commit at test start and sets spry.trunk to it. spry.trunk is first-class explicit config; nothing in sp assumes trunk == default branch. Land tests then ff-push their OWN trunk — no cross-test main contention, no restoreMainToBaseline between tests.
- Per-test unit IDs / branchPrefix (e.g. spry/t<order>/): no two tests share branch names or PRs. Trunk names and unit IDs are pinned PER TEST not per run — they are cassette keys (pr create --base <trunk> is recorded).
- Docs must still tell the main story: register doc.scrub(trunkName -> main) and prefix scrubs in setupDocRepo (step 6) so generated docs are unchanged.
- FIDELITY CAVEAT (do not lose this): GitHub marks a PR MERGED via reachability from the DEFAULT branch only — the property the whole land redesign rests on (beads spry-4k6, docs/rebuild-roadmap.md). Landing onto a side-branch trunk leaves PRs OPEN on GitHub. Keep ONE canonical land test on the real main, serialized under the existing record lock with its main-restore, as the standing validation. Everything else runs lock-free in parallel.
- withGitHubFixture: replay path unchanged; record path becomes suite-start reset (once, before parallel tests start — e.g. a preload/beforeAll gate) + no per-test lock except the canonical land test. Cross-run staleness (branches/PRs from a previous session) is handled by that suite-start reset.
- tests/lib/github-fixture.test.ts exercises reset()/deleteAllBranches/restoreMainToBaseline — repo-wide ops that bulldoze concurrent tests regardless of namespacing. Either fence them (run under the exclusive lock before/after the parallel phase) or point them at a second dedicated repo (SPRY_TEST_REPO_NAME=spry-check-fixture via scripts/setup-spry-check.ts) — second option preferred, it deletes most lock choreography from the doc-test path.
- GitHub API burst rate limits under concurrency: watch for secondary-rate-limit responses during recording; throttle concurrency if hit (bun test --max-concurrency).

VERIFY: full pre-merge gate green with SPRY_RECORD=1 bun test --concurrent; record wall clock drops from sum-of-CI-waits to roughly the slowest single test; docs/generated byte-identical to the serial recording; the canonical main-trunk land test still records real MERGED behavior.
close_reason: Implemented and committed (f475277): per-test trunk/prefix/refs namespaces via setupDocRepo, suite-start-only reset + exclusive canonical land test in withGitHubFixture, fixture unit tests moved to dedicated spry-check-fixture repo, all 8 cassettes re-recorded concurrently (153s vs ~320s serial), docs byte-identical through record + double playback. MERGED-semantics discovery filed as spry-tm2l.

title: Discovery: Bun 1.3.11 --concurrent flakily hangs a test in tests/git/config.test.ts (lost subprocess completion)
description: While making the suite green under bun test --concurrent (spry-qfc8.2), tests/git/config.test.ts flakily hung: 1-3 arbitrary tests never complete (full per-test timeout, ~0 CPU; process idle in kevent64 with no child processes). Repro: loop 'bun test --concurrent tests/git/config.test.ts' — ~4-5 hangs per 15 runs. Ruled out: per-test await import('bun') (hoisted, still hangs); Bun.$ shell (converted config writes to the Bun.spawn-based runner, still hangs); --max-concurrency=8 (still hangs); describe.serial (Bun 1.3.11 type-declares it but the runner IGNORES it — verified with a sleep-ordering scratch test). Hanging tests always exercise fast-failing git commands (git config --get on unset keys: exit 1, empty output), suggesting a lost stream-close/exit event race in Bun for fast-exiting subprocesses under concurrent load. Control: tests/git/queries.test.ts (same shape, no failing git calls) never hung in 10/10. WORKAROUND SHIPPED: tests/lib/serial.ts serialChain() wraps every async test body in config.test.ts (and github-fixture-wrapper.test.ts); 20/20 clean after. RECURRENCE: same signature hit tests/git/status.test.ts during the spry-qfc8 final review (spry-6qnm) — every recurrence costs a diagnosis. ACTION (2026-07-14): Bun 1.3.14 is out (installed: 1.3.11). The upgrade experiment is ~10 minutes: bump Bun, remove serialChain from config.test.ts, loop 'bun test --concurrent tests/git/config.test.ts' 15-20x (historic rate: 4-5 hangs per 15 runs), and test whether describe.serial now works. If fixed: delete serial.ts uses and close this + spry-6qnm; if not: re-wrap, keep the pin, report upstream.

title: Discovery: cassette re-record churn from GitHub Actions check-run state (statusCheckRollup)
description:
Found during spry-qfc8.4 (cassette hygiene / zero-churn gate). After normalizing PR numbers and stripping options to stdin at record time (src/lib/recording-client.ts), two back-to-back SPRY_RECORD=1 full-suite runs still differ in 6 of 8 cassettes (commands__sync--020/022/025/060/070.json, commands__group--025.json) — exclusively in the statusCheckRollup.contexts.nodes arrays inside recorded graphql stdout: CheckRun count varies and status/conclusion flips between QUEUED/IN_PROGRESS/null and COMPLETED/SUCCESS. Cause: sync/group doc tests query PR status milliseconds after pushing, capturing in-flight CI. (The accumulation half — repeated SHAs across sessions collecting check runs — was FIXED by spry-qfc8.5's per-run seeded dates; the mid-flight timing half remains.) Land cassettes are byte-identical because land waits for checks to settle.

PREFERRED FIX (added 2026-07-14 after epic review): scope the spry-check repo's GitHub Actions workflow to run ONLY for the land namespaces — a pull_request branches filter (matches BASE branch) covering main, trunk/land-*, and the land stacks' unit-branch bases spry/t-land-*/** (the upper PR of a stack bases on the unit branch below it, NOT the trunk — omit these and land's waitForChecks/readiness gate starves). Task 7's per-test namespaces make this precise. Sync/group PRs then trigger zero check runs, so their recorded statusCheckRollup is deterministically empty — REAL data from a repo that genuinely runs no CI for those branches, not fabrication. Land keeps real checks and is already byte-stable. Implement in the workflow YAML managed by scripts/setup-spry-check.ts (keep the bootstrap reproducible) + apply to the live repo. Expect a one-time cassette re-baseline; sync/group doc output shows no CI glyphs (pushed / Created PR / Sync complete) so no docs change expected — verify via the gate. Result: AGENTS.md gate wording goes back to 'Expected churn: NONE'.

Rejected/insufficient options kept for the record: (a) per-run dates — done, fixed accumulation only; (b) canonicalize rollups at record time — fabricates recorded data, interacts with sp view/land readiness semantics; (c) sync/group wait for CI to settle — IMPOSSIBLE: the rollup query happens inside the same sp invocation that pushes the branch; there is no seam between push and query to wait in.

Verify with: SPRY_RECORD=1 bun test twice, diff tests/fixtures/cassettes/ — expect byte-identical.

title: Discovery: GitHub marks PRs MERGED on base-branch reachability, not default-branch reachability — upper stacked PRs stay OPEN after sp land
description:
Observed live during spry-qfc8.7's concurrent record run (2026-07-13) on happycollision/spry-check. After 'sp land --through <top>' ff-pushed the trunk: (a) trunk=main: PR 1285 (head spry/t-land-010/aaa11111, base main) flipped MERGED; PR 1286 (head spry/t-land-010/bbb22222, base spry/t-land-010/aaa11111) stayed OPEN — still OPEN 25+ minutes later, so not replication lag. (b) trunk=trunk/land-020 (a NON-default side branch): PR 1287 (base trunk/land-020) ALSO flipped MERGED; PR 1288 (stacked base) stayed OPEN. So the operative rule is: GitHub marks a PR MERGED when a push to its BASE branch makes the head reachable from the base — the default branch is not special, and reachability from the default branch alone does NOT flip stacked-upper PRs.

WHERE THE WRONG CLAIM LIVES: docs/rebuild-roadmap.md ('GitHub marks a PR MERGED on reachability from the default branch, not its base' — two places: the 'sp land was redesigned' bullet and the 'sp land does not retarget PR bases' bullet, citing beads spry-4k6 and spry-dzp) and the generated land doc prose (docs/generated/commands/land.md, produced by tests/commands/land.doc.test.ts order 10).

WHERE THE CODEBASE ALREADY KNOWS THE CORRECT RULE (internal contradiction worth noting): src/commands/sync.ts — the reorder-safe park logic comment (~line 208-213) says 'an in-place force-push can make a PR's head reachable from its stale base and GitHub marks it MERGED', and the parkMismatchedToTrunk jsdoc (~line 530-553) states outright: 'GitHub decides MERGED by commit-SHA reachability from the base branch tip'. So sync's accidental-merge defense was built on the correct (base-reachability) model while land's design rationale was written against the wrong (default-branch) model. The two never collided because land only ff-pushes trunk, and bottom PRs are based on trunk.

EVIDENCE ARTIFACTS: cassettes tests/fixtures/cassettes/commands__land--010.json (canonical, trunk=main, records the gh pr view MERGED poll) and commands__land--020.json (side-trunk trunk/land-020 land). The canonical land doc test (tests/commands/land.doc.test.ts order 10, added in spry-qfc8.7 commit f475277) polls gh pr view for the BOTTOM PR only and asserts MERGED, with a comment recording this behavior. Historical support: pre-task-7 recordings left the upper spry/dondenton/bbb22222 PR (#1276) CLOSED-by-reset, never MERGED — the default-branch claim was apparently never validated for stacked uppers.

IMPACT: after sp land, upper stacked PRs remain OPEN on GitHub until something advances or deletes their base branch. Users may see 'open' PRs for landed work. Plausible interaction (UNVERIFIED): deleting a landed base branch (sp clean, or spry.autoDeleteOnLand) causes GitHub to retarget the open upper PR to the default branch; whether that retarget triggers merge detection (head now reachable from new base) or leaves it OPEN is unknown and is the key experiment to run.

NEEDS: (1) verify what base-branch deletion (sp clean / autoDeleteOnLand) does to those OPEN upper PRs — retarget to default? flip MERGED? stay OPEN?; (2) decide whether sp land should do something (document, advance stacked bases post-land, or rely on branch deletion); (3) correct docs/rebuild-roadmap.md and the land doc-test prose; (4) revisit beads spry-4k6/spry-dzp record so the design history isn't silently wrong. Related: the whole spry-qfc8.7 per-test-trunk scheme RELIES on side-trunk lands flipping MERGED (observation b) — it works today; if GitHub ever changes this, the lock-free land test loses fidelity.

title: Discovery: Bun 1.3.11 --concurrent lost-subprocess hang also hits tests/git/status.test.ts
description: During the spry-qfc8 final whole-branch review, one full 'bun run test:concurrent' run timed out a single test: tests/git/status.test.ts > getWorkingTreeStatus > staged changes: hasStagedChanges true (120000ms, suite otherwise 616 pass). Immediate serial re-run of the file: 7/7 pass in ~1s; immediate full concurrent re-run: 617 pass 0 fail in 56s. Signature matches spry-ojjj (Bun 1.3.11 loses completion of fast-exiting git subprocesses under --concurrent; previously observed in tests/git/config.test.ts, worked around there with serialChain). status.test.ts runs fast 'git status --porcelain' subprocesses per test and has no serialChain. Datapoint for spry-ojjj: the bug is not confined to config.test.ts; any fast-subprocess-heavy file can flake. If it recurs, apply the same serialChain workaround to status.test.ts; longer term, revisit on newer Bun per spry-ojjj.

title: Bug: parallel record mode leaks cross-test SHAs into doc fragments — SHA_POOL exhausted in docs:build
description:
Found during the spry-qfc8 pre-merge record gate (2026-07-14, HEAD 0d0e05e). SPRY_RECORD=1 bun test --concurrent passed clean (614 pass / 8 skip / 0 fail, 153.9s), but the subsequent 'bun run docs:build' crashed: 'SHA_POOL exhausted — 19 unique SHAs needed but pool only has 18 entries' (tests/lib/sha-scanner.ts buildShaMap, called per-fragment from scripts/build-docs.ts scrubFragment).

The overflowing fragment is .test-tmp/doc-fragments/commands__sync--060.json ('Pushing every tracked stack with --all', tests/commands/sync.doc.test.ts order 60). Verified via 'gh api repos/happycollision/spry-check/git/commits/<sha>' that its 19 collected SHAs include commits minted by OTHER doc tests: 'Add login', 'Add session handling', 'Add login form' (land/group tests), 'B'/'A' (sync order-70), plus 5 'update pr cache' commits and the spry-check init commit. Only ~6 belong to this test.

Root cause: tests/lib/doc.ts (~lines 129-176) collects scrub SHAs by running 'git log --all --reflog' on BOTH the doc repo and repo.originPath. Under task spry-qfc8.7's parallel record mode the origin is a shared spry-check clone, so the walk sweeps whatever refs OTHER concurrently-recording tests have pushed at scrub time. The count is therefore racy run-to-run; this run it hit 19 and tripped the 18-entry pool guard.

Notably, NONE of the 19 SHAs appear in the fragment's rendered entries (verified by scanning entry content for all prefixes >=6 chars), so the docs output itself is unaffected — the crash is purely the capacity guard on racy input. Fix directions to evaluate: (a) scope the origin walk to the test's own per-test trunk/branch refs instead of --all on the shared clone, (b) only register SHAs that actually appear in the fragment's entry content, or (c) grow SHA_POOL — but (c) leaves the underlying race and slot-assignment instability warned about in sha-scanner.ts's comment (assignment is encounter-order, so racy input ordering could still churn docs for fragments that DO render SHAs). Evidence fragment preserved during the gate session; reproduce by re-running the pre-merge gate in AGENTS.md.
close_reason: Fixed in 5a63a4f (branch claude/spry-qfc8-subagent-dev-e91e14). docTest's scrub-SHA scan now runs 'git remote remove origin' on the doc repo before its 'git log --all --reflog' walks, deleting all remote-tracking refs AND their reflogs (incl. the origin/HEAD symref) in one stroke — the refs/remotes reflogs were the reason --exclude=refs/remotes/* could not work (verified empirically: --reflog still walks them). Discovery is now scoped to the test's own work (local branches, HEAD/refs-heads reflogs which fetch never writes, local refs/spry/*, origin-side walk); the mutation runs after the test body on a repo torn down right after. Verified: two full record runs (614 pass/0 fail, 140.6s/137.9s) under different scheduling produced byte-identical per-fragment SHA counts (sync--060 now 13 own SHAs, was a racy 19 incl. ~13 foreign); docs/generated byte-identical through record + double playback (617 pass/0 fail twice); residual cassette churn was only the known spry-76lu statusCheckRollup class. Also confirmed the gate runner's anomaly: suite-start reset 'main restored: false' is the benign already-at-baseline path (the prior canonical land test restores main in its exclusive finally; the true branch only fires after a land crash between ff-push and restore).

title: Inject output sink into commands; stop patching console/process.exit in tests
description:
Follow-up to spry-qfc8.2. Commands write via process-global console.log/console.error and bail via monkeypatched process.exit (with @ts-ignore), so ~70 tests capture output by patching those globals under an in-process mutex (tests/lib/capture.ts captureLogs/trapExit) — which serializes all capturing tests (why concurrent replay floors at ~55s), makes queue-wait count against each test's own timeout (the ONLY reason test:concurrent needs --timeout 120000), and carries a deadlock footgun if a restore() misses a finally.

DESIGN (sharpened 2026-07-14 from cross-session review): the codebase already dependency-injects git and gh through SpryContext — console and exit are the only effects left un-seamed. (1) Add out/err writers to SpryContext (default: console). (2) Replace the ~15 deep process.exit(1) calls with a thrown ExitError that src/cli/index.ts catches — it already sets process.exitCode — rather than threading return codes through every intermediate caller. Then a capturing test constructs a ctx with array-backed writers: no globals, no mutex, no exit trap, no raised timeout, and the ~70 capturing tests run genuinely concurrent. The sweep is mechanical — the nested printing helpers (pushExistingBranches etc.) already receive ctx. TUI paths unaffected (they capture via the PTY driver).

SEQUENCING: this removes the raised-timeout rationale for the test/test:concurrent split; after it lands, the only thing keeping serial-as-default is the Bun runner bug (spry-ojjj/spry-6qnm) — fix both and flip bun test to concurrent by default. Raised by don 2026-07-14 ('should we just write our own logging functions with injected deps?' — yes).

title: Namespace remote spry refs by branchPrefix: refs/spry/<prefix>/{prs,groups}; retire SPRY_REMOTE_REFS_PREFIX seam
description:
Design direction from don (2026-07-14), refined via cross-session review. Task 7 added src/lib/refs-seam.ts (SPRY_REMOTE_REFS_PREFIX env, identity when unset) so parallel record-mode tests don't collide on the repo-wide refs/spry/prs and refs/spry/groups remote refs — a test-only seam whose own doc comment describes the SAME collision at human frequency: every collaborator's sp sync fetches and best-effort pushes the same two refs, with non-fast-forward rejections surfacing as benign-looking warnings.

PRIMARY DESIGN — namespace by BRANCH PREFIX: refs/spry/<encoded-branchPrefix>/{prs,groups}. The PR cache and group records are per-USER data (keyed to the user's branches under spry.branchPrefix), stored today at a repo-global ref. Prefix-namespacing fixes the real collaborator collision (two users targeting the SAME trunk stop clobbering each other — trunk-namespacing would NOT fix this, both would be in trunk_main) AND gives tests isolation for free (task 7 already mints per-test prefixes), so refs-seam.ts, its test, and the SPRY_REMOTE_REFS_PREFIX threading in tests/lib/doc-repo.ts all get deleted. Considered-and-subsumed alternative (don's original sketch): namespace by trunk (refs/spry/trunk_main/prs) — semantically appealing but doesn't address the writer collision (writers are users, not trunks), and one user's multi-trunk stacks already coexist fine under a single prefix ref because cache entries are branch-keyed.

DESIGN QUESTIONS: (1) encoding — branchPrefix contains slashes (spry/dondenton); pick a reversible scheme (e.g. replace / with _ or url-escape) and document it. (2) scope — remote side only (what the seam remaps today); local refs/spry/* likely stays (refs/spry/local/tracked-branches is machine-local, no collision). (3) migration — existing users have refs/spry/prs on their remotes; read-old-write-new fallback or one-shot migration on first sync; sp clean must know both layouts. (4) sp land post-land scrub + sp clean target the right namespace; tests/lib/github-fixture.ts purgeSpryRefs must purge all namespaces. Files: src/gh/pr-cache.ts, src/git/group-titles.ts, src/lib/refs-seam.ts (delete), tests/lib/doc-repo.ts.

Related follow-on (separate small bead spry-?): narrowed fetch refspec in setupDocRepo — with prefix-namespaced refs, record-mode clones can also scope remote.origin.fetch to the test's own namespace, making the scrubber's scan-time 'git remote remove origin' (the spry-4zs6 fix) deletable.

title: setupDocRepo: narrow record-mode clone fetch refspec to the test's own namespace
description: Optional hardening on top of the spry-4zs6 fix (cross-session review, 2026-07-14). The shipped fix — git remote remove origin in tests/lib/doc.ts before the scrub-SHA scan — is proven byte-identical across record runs, but it's symptom-side: sibling tests' branches still enter the record-mode clone via sp sync's git fetch (default refspec fetches everything) and are visible to anything enumerating refs DURING the test body; only the scan is protected. More causal: in tests/lib/doc-repo.ts setupDocRepo (record mode), set the clone's remote.origin.fetch refspec to only this test's namespace — its trunk (trunk/<leaf>-<order>, or the default branch for the canonical land test), its branch prefix (spry/t-<leaf>-<order>/*), and its remapped refs/spry namespace. Foreign commits then never enter the repo: deterministic repo state throughout the test, less fetch traffic, and the scan-time remote-remove (and its long comment block about --exclude vs remote-tracking reflogs) becomes deletable. Note: git updates remote-tracking refs on push too, but those are the test's own branches — namespace holds. Do together with or after spry-58q9 (prefix-namespaced refs change what the refspec must cover). Acceptance: full record gate byte-identical, twice, with the remote-remove deleted.

title: Generate doc-scrub placeholder pools instead of hand-maintaining finite lists
description: From cross-session review (2026-07-14). tests/lib/sha-scanner.ts maps discovered SHAs first-seen -> slots in a finite hand-written pool; 'SHA_POOL exhausted — N needed but pool only has 18 entries' (the spry-4zs6 failure signature) is a failure mode that exists ONLY because the pool is a hand-maintained list. Replace with a deterministic generator — fakeSha(i) -> 40 stable hex chars derived from the index (and same for any other finite pools in the scrubber) — which can never exhaust. Seed/shape the generator to reproduce the current 18 committed entries to avoid a docs re-baseline; otherwise accept a one-time re-baseline proven by the gate (record once, playback twice, byte-identical). Keep the exhaustion error path only if a hard cap is retained for sanity (e.g. warn at absurd counts, which now indicate a leak like spry-4zs6 rather than a small pool).

title: Discovery: recording-client overwrites cassette per sp invocation; only last invocation's gh traffic survives
description: Pre-existing on main (verified: main's committed commands__sync--070.json has the same shape); NOT introduced by the spry-qfc8 branch. createRecordingClient (src/lib/recording-client.ts) starts each sp process with an empty entries array, and persist() rewrites the whole cassette file. When a doc test invokes the sp binary multiple times under one cassette env (tests/commands/sync.doc.test.ts orders 60 and 70 call runSp 2-3 times), each invocation is a fresh process, so only the LAST invocation's gh traffic survives in the committed cassette — commands__sync--070.json contains no 'pr create' entries despite the test's first invocation being 'sync --open aaa11111,bbb22222'. Replay passes only because each replaying process gets a fresh consumed[] over the full cassette, earlier invocations' pr-create misses are swallowed by sync.ts (~:499-502, 'Failed to create PR for <branch>' warning path), and the setup runSp exit codes are unchecked. Consequence: record and replay exercise DIFFERENT code paths for non-final invocations, a latent divergence. Candidate fixes: recording client appends to an existing cassette when the env marks a continuation, or cassette env varies per invocation, or doc tests avoid multi-invocation setups. Related test gap filed separately: sync doc tests should assert setup runSp exit codes.

title: Discovery: sync doc tests don't assert setup runSp exit codes; sp sync downgrades gh failures to warnings, masking stale cassettes
description: Found during spry-qfc8 review-fix work (2026-07-15). Two related gaps: (1) tests/commands/sync.doc.test.ts setup invocations (runSp at ~:349, ~:418 in orders 60/70) never check the returned exit code, so a setup sync that failed (e.g. unmatched cassette entry in replay) goes unnoticed. (2) sp sync's findPRsForBranches (src/commands/sync.ts ~:159-162 catch) downgrades gh errors to a dimmed retargeting-fallback warning, which meant order 70 PASSED against a knowingly-stale cassette during the Task-6 namespace-key change while the other 7 fixture tests correctly failed - the doc test cannot detect its own cassette being stale. Fix: assert exit code 0 on every setup runSp, and consider a doc-test-level assertion that no 'Could not' / fallback-warning lines appear in captured output unless the test intends them.

title: Migrate 12 test files' hand-rolled repos[]/afterAll cleanup blocks onto repoManager()
description: From spry-qfc8 branch review (2026-07-15, verified): tests/commands/{sync,land,clean,rebase,group,stack-analysis}.test.ts, tests/gh/{push,pr-cache}.test.ts, tests/git/{behind,group-titles,tracked-branches}.test.ts, and tests/lib/repo.test.ts each carry an identical copy-pasted block: 'const repos: TestRepo[] = []' + the 'afterAll, not afterEach: under --concurrent a per-test cleanup hook would delete repos out from under still-running sibling tests' comment + the while/pop afterAll loop. tests/lib/repo-manager.ts repoManager() encapsulates exactly this (same afterAll timing, same rationale in its docstring), and the same branch already migrated 6 files onto it (view.test.ts, tests/git/{plumbing,rebase,config,queries,conflict}.test.ts). Mechanical: replace each block with 'const repos = repoManager()'. A future cleanup-policy change currently needs 12 hand-edits.

title: Consolidate exit-trap and console-capture duplicates onto tests/lib/capture.ts helpers
description: From spry-qfc8 branch review (2026-07-15, verified): (1) nine test bodies in tests/commands/sync.test.ts (lines ~409/470/532/667/697/754/782/808/860 at review time) hand-roll process.exit patching with a '__exit:<code>' sentinel although the same file imports trapExit from tests/lib/capture.ts and uses it elsewhere; migration is mechanical (swap catch filter to e.message !== 'process.exit', pattern already at sync.test.ts ~:937). (2) tests/commands/view.test.ts captureView re-implements captureLogs+trapExit by hand and is the only reason capture.ts exports the raw acquireOutputLock primitive; port it onto a composed helper (e.g. captureCommand() returning {out, err, exitCode, restore}) and stop exporting acquireOutputLock so the safe API is the only API. (3) optional: serialChain (tests/lib/serial.ts) and acquireOutputLock (tests/lib/capture.ts) are two promise-tail mutex implementations; one primitive could serve both (~15 lines each, modest payoff).

title: Design: inject output sinks via SpryContext to remove global console patching and test serialization
description: From spry-qfc8 branch review (2026-07-15). tests/lib/capture.ts serializes ALL output-capturing tests (~72+ call sites across sync/land/rebase/clean/view tests) behind one process-global mutex because commands write via bare console.log/console.error and tests must patch globals. This makes captured tests the wall-clock floor under bun test --concurrent and forces the raised 120s timeout in test:concurrent (queue wait counts against each test's own timeout; a bare 'bun test --concurrent' can spuriously time out). Deeper fix, verified feasible: SpryContext (src/lib/context.ts, currently {git, gh}) is already threaded into every command entry point (syncCommand, landCommand, rebaseCommand, cleanCommand, viewCommand, groupCommand), and the ~109 console call sites are concentrated in src/commands/*. Add log/error/exit sinks to ctx (defaulting to console/process.exit), have tests capture per-context - no global patching, no mutex, no serialization, and the lock-wait diagnostic machinery added on the spry-qfc8 branch becomes deletable. Sizeable but mechanical; big concurrent-suite wall-clock win.

title: Make doc-fragment writes atomic (write-temp-then-rename); collapse waitForFragment retry loop
description: From spry-qfc8 branch review (2026-07-15, verified): tests/lib/doc.ts (~:205 at review time) persists fragments with a plain non-atomic Bun.write, and the torn-read hazard is compensated in ONE consumer (tests/lib/doc.test.ts waitForFragment's JSON.parse retry loop, whose own comment admits 'a racing reader can catch a truncated file'). scripts/build-docs.ts buildDocsFromDisk does a bare JSON.parse with no retry - safe today only because docs:build never overlaps a test run in documented workflows. Fix at the writer: write to a temp path then rename into fragmentPath (rename is atomic on the same filesystem); then waitForFragment collapses to a plain existence wait and every future reader is safe by construction.

title: Generalize setupDocRepo PR-number scrub; cover land.ts bare #N form
description: From spry-qfc8 branch review (2026-07-15, verified): tests/lib/doc-repo.ts's PR-number canonicalization enumerates message-specific patterns ('Created PR #', 'parked PR #', 'retargeted PR #', pull/<n> URLs) and is already incomplete: src/commands/sync.ts emits 'Could not park PR #<n>' (~:577) and 'Could not retarget PR #<n>' (~:608) which don't match ('parked PR #' != 'park PR #'), and src/commands/land.ts (~:91) emits a bare '#<n>' with no 'PR ' prefix at all. No current doc test captures those forms (they arise on gh-error paths that would fail happy-path assertions first), so this is latent - but the first doc test that documents an error/readiness path leaks a raw GitHub-minted number into docs/generated as nondeterministic churn, surfacing only at the pre-merge record gate. Fix: replace the enumeration with a generic anchored /PR #\d+/ scrub (keep the URL scrub) and decide how to canonicalize land's bare '#<n>' form (e.g. scrub '#<n>' when adjacent to known land output, or change land.ts to say 'PR #<n>').

title: Record-mode SHA-scan origin walk is a no-op (originPath is an https URL); remote-only commits would leak unscrubbed
description: From spry-qfc8 branch review (2026-07-15, verifier-confirmed mechanism, no current test can trigger it): in record mode tests/lib/repo.ts sets repo.originPath to the fixture's https repoUrl, so tests/lib/doc.ts's origin-side walk ('git --git-dir=<originPath> log --all --reflog') fails and Promise.allSettled swallows it - the walk the code comments cite as the safety net does not exist in record mode; additionally 'git remote remove origin' runs before the clone walk, dropping remote-tracking refs from discovery. Any commit reachable ONLY via remote-tracking refs/origin reflogs (e.g. a GitHub-minted merge commit surfaced by a future fixture.mergePR-style doc test) would have its 40-hex SHA missing from the fragment's scrub registry and leak raw into docs/generated during record runs only. Guard the origin walk (skip when originPath is a URL, or walk the fixture's local clone path instead) or document the constraint where the walk is defined. Latent: verified no current doc test surfaces a remote-only commit; two full record runs produced byte-identical docs.

title: terminal-driver: extract shared raceExit helper; drop dead withSpryCheckRecordLock export; tracked-branches malformed-JSON test
description: Three small verified cleanups from the spry-qfc8 branch review (2026-07-15), batched: (1) tests/lib/terminal-driver.ts implements the exit-vs-timeout Promise.race three times (waitForExit ~:113, close ~:152, a fail-fast variant in waitForText ~:84) with different result shapes; extract one raceExit(timeoutMs) helper. (2) tests/lib/github-fixture.ts withSpryCheckRecordLock (~:457) is exported with a docstring paragraph advertising external callers, but its only call sites are two internal ones in the same file (the fixture unit tests moved to serialChain + the dedicated spry-check-fixture repo); drop the export keyword and the stale consumer-facing sentence. (3) tests/git/tracked-branches.test.ts calls loadTrackedBranches whose console.error catch branch (malformed JSON in the ref blob) is never exercised by any test; add a malformed-JSON case (and wrap it in captureLogs since it emits). All mechanical.

title: Discovery: fixture reset cannot delete PRs; spry-check accumulates stale CLOSED records on reused head branches
description:
Found while running the pre-merge record gate on branch claude/spry-qfc8-subagent-dev-e91e14 (now merged to main as 7482456).

WHAT: The suite-start reset in tests/lib/github-fixture.ts (closeAllPRs) can only CLOSE pull requests — GitHub has no API to delete a PR. Because doc tests reuse deterministic per-test head branch names (e.g. spry/t-commands__sync--020/aaa11111), every recording session leaves another CLOSED PR record on that same head branch in happycollision/spry-check. Verified: at gate time each per-test head branch carried 2 PR records (one OPEN from the current run + one CLOSED from a prior run), and high-traffic branches like spry/dondenton/aaa11111 had 60+ CLOSED/MERGED records.

WHY IT MATTERS: spry's PR-lookup query (src/gh/pr.ts) originally asked for the single most-recently-UPDATED PR on a head branch (first: 1, orderBy UPDATED_AT DESC, no state filter). Closing a stale PR bumps its UPDATED_AT, so a dead CLOSED record could sort ahead of the live PR and become 'the PR' spry cached/displayed. This surfaced as a phantom '✓ Updated PR cache (1 PR)' line reappearing in docs/generated on every fresh record run, failing the gate's docs-stability check.

FIXED (product layer, in the merged branch): src/gh/pr.ts now fetches first: 10 and parsePRResponse prefers the OPEN node; src/commands/sync.ts writePRCache caches OPEN PRs only. This makes spry (and the record gate) robust to residual PRs — the code ignores stale records. So this is NOT a correctness blocker anymore.

REMAINING FOOTGUN (why this chore exists): the fixture reset still accumulates stale PR records on spry-check without bound. It is cosmetic/hygiene today (the product ignores them), but it (a) makes spry-check messy to read/debug, (b) grows the PR-query result set over time, and (c) means the gate depends on the product filter rather than a clean fixture. Options to consider: (1) rotate/uniquify per-session head branch names so residue never collides on one branch; (2) periodically hard-reset spry-check via scripts/setup-spry-check.ts; (3) accept it and document that spry-check is expected to accumulate closed PRs. Low priority — capture only.

title: Discovery: rebaseOntoTrunk is dead code
description: src/git/rebase.ts:155 rebaseOntoTrunk() has no production callers. It is only re-exported at src/git/index.ts:57 and used in tests/git/rebase.test.ts:170-263. sp rebase (src/commands/rebase.ts) reimplements the rebasePlumbing->finalizeRewrite logic inline instead of calling rebaseOntoTrunk. Resolution: either remove the function + its export + tests, or wire sp rebase to use it. Found by a subagent during the scriptable-sp-group (sp group --apply) work on branch claude/sp-group-scripting-59c5f8.

title: Discovery: unify reorder-conflict messaging between sp group and sp rebase
description: src/commands/group.ts's reorder conflict-bail (added in commit f47b08f during the scriptable-sp-group work) dumps raw 'git merge-tree' porcelain on conflict, unlike src/commands/rebase.ts:78-95 which uses parseConflictOutput to print the commit subject, a bulleted file list, and a 'run sp rebase again' remediation hint. rebase.ts already duplicates its conflict-print block twice (lines 78-95 and 164-177); adding a shared printRebaseConflict() helper and using it in group.ts too would satisfy rule-of-three and give consistent UX. Deferred from that task because the plan mandated the exact group.ts string and extraction would have been scope creep. Branch: claude/sp-group-scripting-59c5f8.

title: sp sync: execute prAction:CLOSE intent recorded by sp group --apply
description: sp group --apply (branch claude/sp-group-scripting-59c5f8) records a PR-close intent by marking the local PR-cache entry (refs/spry/prs) state=CLOSED in applyGroupDoc (src/commands/group.ts). But NOTHING in src/commands/sync.ts or src/gh/ currently reads a locally-CLOSED cache entry to actually close the PR on GitHub — no executor exists. So today prAction:CLOSE only removes the PR from local 'open' tracking; the real GitHub PR stays open indefinitely. The design spec assumed 'executed later by sync' but a sync-side executor was never a task in the scriptable-group plan; the CHANGELOG + code comment were softened to say the close is local-only for now. Need: sync detects locally-CLOSED cache entries whose PR is still OPEN on GitHub and closes them (gh pr close), or a dedicated reconcile step. This is the one functional gap keeping --apply's PR-close from being end-to-end. See docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-21-scriptable-sp-group-design.md.

comments.author: don.denton
comments.text: When this is fixed: REMOVE (do not modify) the '--apply PR-close is local-only' caveat paragraph in AGENTS.md's 'Dogfooding sp' section — the paragraph that ends 'Remove this caveat when spry-ifaj is fixed.' Once the sync-side executor actually closes PRs on GitHub, the caveat is no longer true, so delete it entirely rather than editing it.

title: view --json emits id:<short-sha> for commits with no Spry-Commit-Id; PRUnit.id should never be a sha
description:
PRUnit.id uses a short-SHA fallback that leaks a fake identity into a field everything downstream treats as a real Spry-Commit-Id.

ROOT CAUSE: src/parse/stack.ts:51 (in detectPRUnits / parseStack) sets a single-commit unit id as:
    id: commitId || commit.hash.slice(0, 8)
So a commit WITHOUT a Spry-Commit-Id trailer gets id = its 8-char short SHA. (unit.commitIds already tells the truth: it is `commitId ? [commitId] : []`, i.e. empty when there is no real id.)

SYMPTOM (found dogfooding sp group --apply on branch claude/sp-group-scripting-59c5f8, PR #27): `sp view --json` emitted `{"type":"commit","id":"640730d6", "sha":"640730d6...", ...}` for trailerless commits — i.e. the SHORT SHA as the id. buildStackTree (src/parse/stack-tree.ts:32-38) faithfully copies unit.id, so the bad value originates upstream, not in the serializer. Correct JSON for a trailerless commit should be `id: null, sha: <full sha>`. Emitting id:<sha> is actively misleading: a caller round-trips `sp view --json | sp group --apply -` and the doc LOOKS valid but fails reconcile with `Unknown id (not in live stack): 640730d6`, because the sha is not a real live Spry-Commit-Id. (Workaround during dogfooding: run any mutating sp command first — inject happens — then re-read view --json so ids are the real minted 8-hex values, then apply.)

WHY id-as-sha is a category error: PRUnit.id is the stable unit identity — branchForUnit turns it into <prefix>/<id> (src/git/branch.ts), the PR cache (refs/spry/prs) is keyed by it, --through / --open <id> match against it, and group records store it. All assume a MINTED, rebase-stable Spry-Commit-Id. A short SHA is none of that (unstable across rewrites, and conceptually collides with the separate `sha` field).

PROPOSED DIRECTION (explore before committing): make PRUnit.id `string | null` — a unit either has a real Spry-Commit-Id or has no id yet (null); the short-sha becomes purely a `sp view` HUMAN-display fallback (show short sha or "(no id)"), never a real id. buildStackTree then emits id:null (or reads unit.commitIds[i] ?? null). 

SCOPE TO EXPLORE (do NOT assume — map it): every reader of PRUnit.id and whether nullable breaks/needs a guard — branchForUnit, PR cache keying, identifier resolution for --through/--open (IdentifierResolution), group records, parseStack validation, ui/format.ts human view. Expectation is they only ever operate post-inject (so never see null), but VERIFY rather than hope. A narrower JSON-only fix (buildStackTree emits unit.commitIds[i] ?? null, leaving stack.ts:51 and the human view alone) is the minimal alternative if the nullable-id change ripples too far.

RELATED ergonomics note (separate but adjacent): even with correct id:null output, a first `view --json | group --apply -` on a trailerless branch cannot round-trip, because a commit with id:null in an apply doc is a schema error (only new GROUPS may be id:null) and --apply mints ids only via new-group id:null, not for commits. Trailerless branches need an id-injection step first (any mutating sp command does it today). Consider whether `sp view` should inject ids first, or whether the unknown-id/`view` UX should hint at this. Can be a follow-up to this bug.

title: sp land: support creating a merge commit (not only fast-forward)
description:
Allow `sp land` to complete a land by creating an actual MERGE COMMIT on trunk, as an alternative to the current fast-forward-only strategy.

CURRENT BEHAVIOR: `sp land` does a single fast-forward push of `origin/<trunk>` to the target unit tip (src/commands/land.ts:99-100, `pushBranch`). GitHub then marks every in-scope PR MERGED by reachability from the default branch — no merge commit is ever created, and the GitHub merge API is deliberately NOT used (rebuild decision, docs/rebuild-roadmap.md:132). Consequence: a stack that is BEHIND trunk cannot land — the ff push is rejected and surfaces as "behind trunk -> run sp rebase" (roadmap:132). So today landing REQUIRES the stack be fast-forwardable onto trunk (i.e. rebased first).

REQUEST: add a merge-commit land path so land can complete even when trunk has advanced, by creating a real merge commit (git merge --no-ff style, or plumbing: commit-tree with two parents = current trunk tip + stack tip, merged tree) and pushing that to trunk, rather than requiring a fast-forward. This preserves the "sp owns the merge locally, never calls the GitHub merge API" principle (still just a push of a locally-built commit).

OPEN DESIGN QUESTIONS to explore (do not assume):
- Flag/mode surface: `sp land --merge` (opt-in) vs. auto-fallback to merge when ff is not possible vs. a config (spry.landStrategy = ff|merge)? Default should almost certainly stay ff (linear history is the current contract).
- Merge conflicts: a merge commit can conflict (unlike a clean ff). Need conflict prediction/bail BEFORE any push, mirroring the plumbing dry-run approach used for reorder (rebasePlumbing / merge-tree in src/git/plumbing.ts) — land must stay all-or-nothing, never leave a half-done merge.
- Does GitHub still mark the in-scope PRs MERGED by reachability when trunk gains a MERGE commit whose second parent is the stack tip? The stack commits ARE reachable from the new trunk tip via the merge, so reachability-based MERGED detection should still fire — VERIFY this against real GitHub (it is the whole basis of sp land marking PRs merged; roadmap:132-133). If reachability does not flip them, this feature is not viable without the merge API.
- Interaction with post-land scrub (src/commands/land.ts drops PR-cache entries + group records for landed units) and `sp clean` (detects landed as merge-base --is-ancestor of trunk — a merge commit keeps the stack reachable, so clean should still reap, roadmap:135). Confirm both still work.
- Commit message / authorship of the generated merge commit.
- Does this remove the need to rebase-before-land in the common behind-trunk case? That is the main user-facing win.

CONTEXT: found while dogfooding the scriptable-sp-group work (branch claude/sp-group-scripting-59c5f8, PR #27). Relevant prior land design: roadmap decisions at docs/rebuild-roadmap.md:132-136; land impl src/commands/land.ts; conflict-prediction plumbing src/git/plumbing.ts (rebasePlumbing / merge-tree).

title: sp leaves stale group/PR-cache state refs after a PR is landed outside sp (GitHub/manual merge)
description:
After a PR that sp opened is landed by something OTHER than `sp land` (here: a GitHub "Merge pull request" merge commit), sp does not fully reconcile its state — commit detection self-heals but the state refs (refs/spry/groups, refs/spry/prs) keep orphaned entries for the landed group/PR forever, and no command reaps them.

=== EXACTLY WHAT HAPPENED (repro from real dogfooding) ===
Branch: claude/sp-group-scripting-59c5f8 (the scriptable-sp-group feature). Steps:
1. `sp group --apply -` grouped all 36 branch commits into ONE group (minted group id `bcf60195`, title "Scriptable sp group (--apply + view --json)"). This wrote refs/spry/groups (blob keyed `bcf60195`).
2. `sp sync --open bcf60195` pushed branch spry/happycollision/bcf60195 and opened PR #27. This wrote refs/spry/prs (blob keyed `bcf60195`) and pushed both state refs to origin.
3. PR #27 was landed via GitHub, NOT sp land: `gh pr merge 27 --merge --delete-branch`. origin/main advanced fb7246a..22b1e2a where 22b1e2a is a real MERGE COMMIT (2 parents: old trunk fb7246a + stack tip 3d03ea0), PR #27 -> MERGED, remote head branch deleted by --delete-branch.

=== OBSERVED sp STATE AFTER THE EXTERNAL MERGE ===
- `sp view` -> "No commits ahead of origin/main". CORRECT. getStackCommits computes origin/main..HEAD, and the merge made all 36 commits ancestors of origin/main, so the stack reads empty. Commit/stack DETECTION self-heals. GOOD.
- LOCAL refs/spry/groups still has blob `bcf60195`; LOCAL refs/spry/prs still has blob `bcf60195`. STALE — the group no longer corresponds to any live stack unit.
- REMOTE still has refs/spry/groups and refs/spry/prs (contained bcf60195 when pushed in step 2). STALE on the remote too.
- `sp clean --dry-run` -> "No landed branches to clean". sp clean ONLY reaps remote BRANCHES (by merge-base --is-ancestor of trunk); it never touches group records or PR-cache entries. And the head branch was already deleted by --delete-branch, so clean has nothing to do. The stale group/prs refs are left untouched.

=== WHY NOTHING CLEANS IT UP (the gap) ===
- sp land scrubs landed group records + PR-cache entries DETERMINISTICALLY because it knows the scope it just landed (src/commands/land.ts post-land scrub). An EXTERNAL land gives sp no such signal, so scrub never runs.
- The roadmap claims stale state "self-heals" (docs/rebuild-roadmap.md ~ "PR cache rebuilds on the next sp sync, group records on the next sp group"). That is only PARTIALLY true and does NOT cover a FULLY-landed stack: with the stack empty there is nothing left to sync or group to trigger a rebuild, and writePRCache early-returns on an empty cache (roadmap note), so the rebuild never fires. Result: bcf60195 group + prs entries persist indefinitely, local and remote.
- sp clean is branch-only: src/commands/clean.ts imports only deleteRemoteBranch/isAlreadyGone; it enumerates remote-tracking branches under <remote>/<branchPrefix>/* and deletes those that are ancestors of trunk. No group/prs handling.

=== WHAT "CORRECT" WOULD MEAN ===
After an external land, sp should drop the landed groups group-records AND their PR-cache entries (local and remote) for any group/unit whose commits are ALL now ancestors of origin/<trunk> — i.e. detect "landed" by the SAME reachability test sp clean already uses for branches, but apply it to the group/prs state refs too. Must handle: partial landing (some units of a multi-group stack landed, others not), the empty-cache early-return in writePRCache, and pushing the pruned refs to the remote (not just local).

=== DESIGN OPTIONS TO DECIDE LATER (not decided) ===
A) Extend `sp clean` to also reap group records + PR-cache entries whose commits are fully ancestors of trunk (reuse its existing reachability detection). One recovery command; consistent detection; keeps clean as "the reaper for anything sp land did not scrub." Needs remote-ref pruning, not just local.
B) Self-heal inside sp view/sync reconcile: drop fully-landed group/prs entries as a side effect of reading. Automatic, but adds mutation to a currently read-only offline view, and view being offline can only prune LOCAL refs, not remote.
C) Some mix (view prunes local opportunistically; clean does the authoritative local+remote prune).

=== RELATED ===
- sp clean landed-detection: docs/rebuild-roadmap.md ~line 135 (ancestor-of-trunk only; intentionally does NOT reap GitHub squash/rebase merges which make new SHAs — a squash/rebase external land is a HARDER variant of this same problem: the branch commits are NOT ancestors of trunk, so reachability detection fails entirely; note this as an out-of-scope-for-now sub-case unless we want trailer-based detection).
- sp land post-land scrub: src/commands/land.ts.
- Found while dogfooding scriptable-sp-group (PR #27, now merged). Adjacent issues: spry-e93t (view --json id bug), spry-cczm (sp land merge-commit strategy), spry-ifaj (sync-side prAction:CLOSE executor).
